> **Read me first.** Five facts dominate everything below and are easy to miss otherwise:
>
> 1. **Recon is read-only.** This report is the sole deliverable. No source file, no document, no brief, no `REGISTER.yaml` entry, and no commit was modified during recon. Findings that would justify a patch are recorded in ¬ß14 (technical debt inventory), not patched.
> 2. **The G-series briefs (`G0..G9`) are skeleton-state architectural intent, not authored execution briefs.** Each is 40‚Äì46 lines, sections `Goal / Time estimate / Deliverables / Success criteria / Status`. Compared with the precedent of `K8_34_COMBINED_KERNEL_CUTOVER_BRIEF_V2.md` (748 lines, full Phase 0..6 with halt protocols, verification scripts, atomic commit shapes), they lack phase-by-phase commit plans, halt triggers, verification protocols, and rollback strategies. The REGISTER classifies them `lifecycle: AUTHORED`, but the briefs themselves carry `Status: NOT STARTED` headers. They are **primary architectural intent** for GPU compute + movement and the recon treats them as such; the recon does **not** advance them.
> 3. **Navigation is hardcoded `200√ó200` in two unrelated places.** `src/DualFrontier.Application/Loop/GameBootstrap.cs:45‚Äì46` (`MapWidth = 200`, `MapHeight = 200`) and `src/DualFrontier.Systems/Pawn/MovementSystem.cs:31‚Äì32` (same constants, redeclared). No single configuration source exists. `NavGrid` itself takes `(width, height)` parameters and is allocation-only at bootstrap. The dynamic-map-expansion requirement from `docs/IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` is not architecturally accommodated in code today ‚Äî it is named in the reservoir as the single backlog item but is not represented in any LOCKED doc, any G-skeleton, or any code path.
> 4. **A'.5 just closed (commit `fc8ecb6`, 2026-05-14).** Managed `World` is now a test fixture; `NativeWorld` is the sole production storage path; 10 production systems migrated; `ElectricGridSystem`+`ConverterSystem` deleted; `[SystemAccess]` is compile-time only (the runtime isolation guard is gone). Three halts (CAPA-2026-05-13, CAPA-2026-05-14 √ó2) preceded the clean execution; methodology Lessons #7 (transcribe APIs, do not paraphrase) and #8 (prove every intermediate state is valid) were added in the same closure commit. This report inherits Lessons #7+#8 as quality bar ‚Äî code is quoted with file:line, brief intent quoted verbatim, paraphrase reserved for ¬ß15 (synthesis).
> 5. **The compute-pipeline API surface is a stub.** `IModComputePipelineApi` is declared in `src/DualFrontier.Contracts/Modding/`, and `RestrictedModApi.ComputePipelines => null` in production code. The G-series skeletons consume an interface that does not yet have implementation. `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 prescribes the surface; K9 closure shipped `Fields` (a peer sub-API of `IModApi` v3); `ComputePipelines` is reserved, not built. This shapes everything in ¬ß7 and ¬ß9.
>
> All five facts inform ¬ß15 (synthesis); none is itself a synthesis claim.

### 1.1 Recon scope

The brief specified three interlocking deliverables in a single report:

- **Layer A** ‚Äî Architecture-vs-game-assumptions map (¬ß11): where engine code is correctly abstracted vs prematurely specialized to current game scale (`200√ó200` map, `50` pawns, `coreSystems` count).
- **Layer B** ‚Äî Technical debt inventory (¬ß14): orphan files, deleted-type stragglers, stale comments, REGISTER drift, known mismatches (`SpanLease<T>.Pairs` Version=1 vs native init Version=0), per-system test coverage gap from A'.5 closure.
- **Layer C** ‚Äî G/K/A' series interlock map (¬ß9): per-brief lifecycle, dependencies, code-vs-skeleton drift, LOCKED-doc concordance.

Recon also produces (¬ß5) a navigation deep-dive ‚Äî the user designated navigation as the most consequential architectural question on the post-cutover horizon. Other subsystems (¬ß6 scheduler, ¬ß7 native kernel + compute, ¬ß8 Mod API v3, ¬ß10 production systems) are characterized to the depth their relation to navigation requires.

### 1.2 Recon non-goals (per brief ¬ß0.3)

- No architectural recommendations or proposed solutions ‚Äî recon records observations and open questions only. Solutions are deliberation work, after the report.
- No ratification proposal ‚Äî recon is **input** to a potential ratification, not the ratification itself.
- No authorship advancement of G-series skeletons ‚Äî recon maps them; it does not advance them from skeleton to authored-ready.
- No effort estimates for specific cleanup or rework.
- No performance measurements, benchmarks, or profiling ‚Äî `dotnet build/test` was not run; the recon reads source, not behavior.

## Section 2 ‚Äî Methodology context inherited

Three methodology facts shape recon's discipline. Recorded here so any reader of this document ‚Äî including the eventual deliberation that consumes the report as input ‚Äî operates against the same quality bar.

### 2.1 Lessons #7 and #8 (`METHODOLOGY.md` v1.8, 2026-05-14)

Both lessons are quoted verbatim:

> **Lesson #7 ‚Äî A brief that prescribes an API must transcribe the API, not paraphrase it.** When a brief tells the executor to call a constructor, a helper, or a file path, the brief author must open the actual source and copy the real signature into the brief at authoring time. ¬´K2-era registry ready¬ª is a note, not a signature. CAPA-2026-05-13's lesson (¬´read entry-point files in full¬ª) addressed transitional-state comments; this lesson addresses *API surface*. A brief is a contract for mechanical execution; a contract cannot reference an interface it has not read.

> **Lesson #8 ‚Äî A brief that splits a change into N steps must prove each of the N‚àí1 intermediate states is valid.** Before a brief prescribes an incremental sequence, the author must walk each intermediate state and confirm it compiles, passes tests, and is architecturally coherent. If an intermediate state cannot be made valid, the change is not incrementally divisible ‚Äî the atomic unit is larger than one step, and the brief must say so.

> **Corollary (METHODOLOGY).** A brief cannot promise ¬´zero halts¬ª; it can promise ¬´halts before damage¬ª. Three halts on the K8.3+K8.4 milestone, zero harmful commits ‚Äî that is the system working.

Recon applies Lesson #7 throughout: code is quoted with file:line; G-skeleton intent is quoted with `## ¬ßN` references; LOCKED-doc claims are quoted with section IDs. Paraphrase is reserved for ¬ß15 (synthesis) and clearly marked.

Recon applies Lesson #8 to itself: this is one atomic deliverable (one report file). The brief explicitly forbade a multi-phase "Phase 0 / Phase 1 / Phase 2" recon shape because intermediate states (half-read documents, half-mapped skeletons) are not valid deliverables. Either recon ships complete, or it halts cleanly with a HALT_REPORT ‚Äî no third option.

### 2.2 The ¬´data exists or it doesn't¬ª principle (`METHODOLOGY.md` ¬ß7.1, seventh invocation A'.4.5)

Seventh formal invocation (2026-05-12, A'.4.5 closure):

> Pre-A'.4.5 the principle applied to code (production data sources, placeholder fields) but not to documentation governance: a `.md` file could exist in the repository without any structural surface declaring its category, tier, lifecycle, or ownership. ‚Ä¶ A'.4.5 introduces `docs/governance/REGISTER.yaml` as the structural surface: every `.md` file either has a register entry (with category √ó tier √ó lifecycle declared) or matches a pattern in `tools/governance/SCOPE_EXCLUSIONS.yaml`. There is no third option.

The recon's REGISTER cross-check (¬ß3.5, ¬ß14.3) is a structural application of this principle: any document on disk without a REGISTER entry, or any REGISTER entry pointing to a missing path, is a Layer B finding.

### 2.3 The architect-executor split (`METHODOLOGY.md` ¬ß2.1)

Recon is a deliberation-mode work product, not an execution-mode one ‚Äî the brief is the input contract, the report is the output. The report does not author briefs, does not advance milestones, and does not change architecture; it produces ground truth for a subsequent deliberation that may produce briefs or ratifications. This is the same shape as the precedent (`CPP_KERNEL_BRANCH_REPORT.md` informed the K0 cherry-pick deliberation; this report informs whatever architectural deliberation the user undertakes next).

## Section 4 ‚Äî LOCKED architecture map

Per-document summary of what each LOCKED architecture document commits to, focused on facts relevant to the navigation deep-dive (¬ß5), the scheduler analysis (¬ß6), the native kernel + compute boundary (¬ß7), and the G/K-series interlock (¬ß9). Each subsection cites version, key invariants, and where the document touches navigation, GPU compute, or modding.

### 4.1 KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md (REGISTER v1.5, on-disk v1.6)

**Status:** AUTHORITATIVE LOCKED. On-disk version is **v1.6 (2026-05-14)**; REGISTER reports v1.5. This is the A'.5 closure version bump ‚Äî REGISTER frontmatter has not absorbed it.

**K-Lxx invariants (Part 0):**

| # | Decision | Choice |
|---|---|---|
| K-L1 | Native language | C++20, MSVC/GCC/Clang |
| K-L2 | Bindings | Pure P/Invoke to `DualFrontier.Core.Native.dll` |
| K-L3 | Component storage paths | Path Œ± (`unmanaged struct`, NativeWorld) default; Path Œ≤ (managed `class` via `[ManagedStorage]`, mod-side store) per opt-in |
| K-L4 | Type IDs | Explicit registry per-mod registration |
| K-L5 | Bootstrap orchestration | Declarative graph, native, parallel where deps allow |
| K-L6 | Game tick scheduler | Managed (because all systems are mods) |
| K-L7 | Span protocol | Read-only spans + write command batching |
| K-L8 | Component lifetime | Native owns storage, managed holds opaque `IntPtr` |
| K-L9 | Mod parity | Vanilla mods register components and systems through same IModApi as third-party |
| K-L10 | Decision rule | ¬ß8 metrics (GC pause / p99 / long-run drift on weak hardware) |
| **K-L11** | **Production storage backbone** | **NativeWorld single source of truth (Solution A); ManagedWorld retained as test fixture and research artifact only** |

**Post-A'.5 implications added in v1.6 header (verbatim):**

> A'.5 K8.3+K8.4 combined milestone closure 2026-05-14 ‚Äî managed `World` retired from production as `ManagedTestWorld` (test-project fixture only); Power subsystem deleted (electricity deferred to a separate GPU-compute brief); 10 production systems migrated to NativeWorld AcquireSpan/BeginBatch; isolation enforced at compile time (runtime guard removed)

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **No K-L invariant addresses map size, world bounds, or dynamic expansion** ‚Äî the kernel's invariants are storage-shape and scheduling-shape, not spatial-shape. NavGrid sits above this layer.
- **K-L6 implication:** "There is NO ¬´native scheduler¬ª for game tick. Native scheduler exists only for bootstrap orchestration. All system code (vanilla mods + third-party mods) executes managed." Navigation systems are managed; pathfinding runs on the CPU side of the boundary by structural design.
- **K-L9 implication:** "No vanilla privilege." Any future GPU-pathfinding system ships through the same `IModApi` as third-party would. The G7 (`Vanilla.Movement`) skeleton commits to this ‚Äî pathfinding ships as a mod, not as engine code.
- **K8.3+K8.4 closure status:** Part 2 records K8.3 and K8.4 as closed (A'.5 combined). K9 closed earlier (A'.4, 2026-05-11). G-series prerequisites partially met: K9 done; M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 (Vulkan instance/device for rendering) **not** done ‚Äî G0 status header reads `Status: NOT STARTED (prerequisite: K9 closed; M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 closed)`.

**Companion documents asserted:** `METHODOLOGY.md`, `CODING_STANDARDS.md`, `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md`, `RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md`.

### 4.2 MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md v1.7

**Status:** LOCKED. Defines the modding architecture; the surface every mod sees.

**Most consequential recon excerpt ‚Äî `IModApi` v3 surface (¬ß4.6.1, verbatim from disk):**

```csharp
public interface IModApi // v3 ‚Äî extends v2
{
    // ‚îÄ‚îÄ v1 + v2 surface (preserved verbatim) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    // RegisterComponent<T> where T : IComponent (Path Œ±; existing v2)
    // RegisterSystem, Publish, Subscribe,
    // PublishContract, TryGetContract, GetKernelCapabilities,
    // GetOwnManifest, Log

    // ‚îÄ‚îÄ New in v3 ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ

    /// <summary>
    /// Register a Path Œ≤ managed-class component ‚Ä¶
    /// </summary>
    void RegisterManagedComponent<T>() where T : class, IComponent;

    /// <summary>
    /// Field-storage sub-API. Null when the kernel does not support fields
    /// (e.g. K9 not yet landed, or the build runs in CPU-only fallback mode).
    /// </summary>
    IModFieldApi? Fields { get; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Compute-pipeline sub-API. Null when the kernel does not support GPU
    /// compute (e.g. G0 not yet landed, or Vulkan 1.3 compute unavailable on
    /// the host machine). Mods checking this for null fall back to CPU
    /// reference implementations.
    /// </summary>
    IModComputePipelineApi? ComputePipelines { get; }
}

public interface IModFieldApi
{
    FieldHandle<T> RegisterField<T>(string id, int width, int height) where T : unmanaged;
    FieldHandle<T> GetField<T>(string id) where T : unmanaged;
}

public interface IModComputePipelineApi
{
    ComputePipelineHandle RegisterPipeline(string id, byte[] spirvBytes);
    ComputePipelineHandle GetPipeline(string id);
}

public sealed class FieldHandle<T> where T : unmanaged
{
    public string Id { get; }
    public int Width { get; }
    public int Height { get; }

    public T ReadCell(int x, int y);
    public void WriteCell(int x, int y, T value);
    public ReadOnlySpan<T> AcquireSpan(out int width, out int height);

    public void SetConductivity(int x, int y, float value);
    public void SetStorageFlag(int x, int y, bool enabled);

    public void DispatchCompute(
        ComputePipelineHandle pipeline,
        ReadOnlySpan<byte> pushConstants,
        int iterations);
}
```

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **`Fields` and `ComputePipelines` are both nullable.** `Fields` is non-null when K9 is built; `ComputePipelines` is non-null when G0 is built. Mods are expected to check for null and degrade. The mod startup example (¬ß4.6.4) shows the canonical pattern.
- **A mod registers a field with `(string id, int width, int height)`.** Width and height are mod-controlled, not engine-controlled. A `200√ó200` field is a mod-chosen size; the architecture does not impose it. This is consistent with the engine-vs-mod placement principle (`GPU_COMPUTE.md` "Engine vs mod placement").
- **`SetConductivity(x, y, float)`** on `FieldHandle<T>` is the navigation-relevant entry ‚Äî a flow-field-driven movement mod (G7) would use this to set walkable-vs-blocked terrain per-cell. The interface is registered but the K9 implementation surfaces only the CPU functional path (per `FIELDS.md` Live v0.1).
- **Cross-mod field access uses dependency declaration** ‚Äî `IModFieldApi.GetField<T>(string id)` requires the calling mod to declare `mod.{owner-id}.field.read:{fieldId}` capability and list the owning mod in `dependencies`. This is consistent with the ¬ß5 type-sharing protocol and ¬ß3 capability model.

### 4.3 RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md v1.0 (characterized, not deep-read)

Defines the runtime layer ‚Äî render, presentation bridge, frame-budget allocation. v1.0 (LOCKED 2026-05-06 per REGISTER). M9.0‚ÄìM9.8 are the runtime layer's milestone numbering. M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 (Vulkan instance/device live) are **prerequisite** for G0 per the G0 skeleton brief's status header. M9.5 is the parallel-development cutover point. M9.8 is Godot deletion.

**Recon-relevant fact:** RUNTIME and KERNEL are companion documents per `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` Part 0. KERNEL Part 0 reads (verbatim): "Combined —Å RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md (M9.0-M9.8) + GPU_COMPUTE.md (–ö9 + G0‚ÄìG9): **15-25 weeks** –¥–ª—è full architectural foundation." The G0 prerequisite chain therefore requires the runtime layer's M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 to land ‚Äî which the current `ROADMAP.md` lists as `‚è≠ Pending`.

### 4.4 MIGRATION_PLAN_KERNEL_TO_VANILLA.md v1.1 (characterized)

Defines K-series ‚Üí M-series progression. v1.1 (post-K-L3.1 amendment, 2026-05-10). Per A'.5 closure brief v2.0 ¬ß9.1, will bump to v1.2 with the K8.3+K8.4 row closures recorded. **No deep-read performed** ‚Äî the recon-relevant content is the K-row status table, and that is reflected in `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` Part 2 (same source of truth).

### 4.5 GPU_COMPUTE.md v2.0 (deep-read ‚Äî primary for navigation)

**Status:** LOCKED v2.0 (2026-05-06). Supersedes Phase 3 `GPU_COMPUTE.md`. **The single most consequential document for the navigation deep-dive.**

The full document is `907` lines. Recon read it end-to-end. Key facts directly relevant to navigation:

#### 4.5.1 Two compute domains (¬ß"Two compute domains")

- **Domain A ‚Äî Field updates (primary, foundational).** Spatial scalar/vector fields stored as dense 2D grids. Each field cell updates per tick via diffusion, propagation, decay. Examples: mana density, electricity field, water pressure, temperature distribution. **Storage:** `RawTileField<T>` in native kernel (K9).
- **Domain B ‚Äî Entity-keyed bulk computation (secondary, opportunistic).** Per-entity calculations that scale poorly on CPU at high entity counts. Original `ProjectileSystem` case. **Storage:** existing `RawComponentStore` (sparse-set). Threshold-driven (CPU below ~500 entities, GPU above).

#### 4.5.2 Flow field pathfinding (verbatim from ¬ß"Flow field pathfinding (Domain A extension)")

> Pathfinding is mathematically isomorphic to electricity propagation: a target spike, an anisotropic propagation respecting walkable terrain, an agent reading the gradient. Same shader template, same field infrastructure, different gameplay interpretation. Adds pathfinding capability to Dual Frontier without expanding the architectural surface ‚Äî a structural pattern unification between pathfinding and supply networks.

Scaling comparison (verbatim):

> - 50 pawns going to 5 work zones ‚Üí A*: 50 searches per tick worst case. Flow field: 5 fields shared, 50 cheap reads.
> - 200 pawns going to 5 work zones ‚Üí A*: 200 searches (linear pain). Flow field: still 5 fields, 200 cheap reads.
> - Cost decouples from pawn count.

#### 4.5.3 Two distance-field options (¬ß"Distance field math")

- **Option A ‚Äî Eikonal equation** (geodesic-accurate distances). GLSL Godunov-upwind sketch ~30 LOC (verbatim in doc). Fast Sweeping Method (FSM) or Fast Marching Method (FMM). "5‚Äì10 sweep passes converge for 200√ó200 grid. Microseconds on mid-range GPU."
- **Option B ‚Äî Simple diffusion with decay** (gameplay-acceptable approximation). Cheaper, ~99% gameplay-equivalent for colony sim.
- **Recommendation in ¬ß"Distance field math":** "Option B initially; upgrade to Option A only if measurement shows gameplay degradation (G9 milestone below)."

#### 4.5.4 Hybrid pathfinding architecture (¬ß"When flow field wins, when A* still needed")

Verbatim:

> Hybrid is the right answer for Dual Frontier:
>
> ```
> Flow fields:
>   - "go to work zone X" (shared by all workers in zone)
>   - "go to escape exit" (shared during emergencies)
>   - "go to dining hall" (shared during meal time)
>   - Common destinations (~5-20 fields active)
>
> A*:
>   - Specific entity targets (this medic to that wounded pawn)
>   - Edge cases (rare unique paths)
>   - Validation (verify flow field gradient leads to reachable target)
> ```
>
> Architectural pattern: flow field default, fall back to A* only when a specific destination is not covered by an active field. Most pawn movement covered by flow fields; A* a fraction.

#### 4.5.5 Engine vs mod placement (¬ß"Engine vs mod placement")

Verbatim:

> **Engine provides infrastructure:**
> - Flow field as a field type (just `Vector2`-typed `RawTileField`)
> - Standard gradient extraction shader (computes ‚àá from distance field)
> - Standard FSM / diffusion shaders for distance field computation
>
> **Mods provide gameplay:**
> - `Vanilla.Movement` defines pawns following flow fields
> - `Vanilla.Movement` registers flow field types per target category
> - `Vanilla.Movement` decides when to recompute fields (target changes, terrain changes, periodic refresh)
>
> This preserves "vanilla = mods" (K-L9). Engine doesn't know about pathfinding semantics; it just provides the field abstraction + compute primitives.

#### 4.5.6 G-series roadmap (¬ß"Roadmap")

GPU_COMPUTE.md inlines the G-series roadmap (G0..G9) at sub-section granularity. Each entry is one paragraph + bullet list ‚Äî essentially the same content as the G-skeleton briefs at `tools/briefs/G*_*.md`. **The G-skeleton briefs are downstream of `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0** ‚Äî they reference ¬ßG{N} of GPU_COMPUTE for the LOCKED architectural intent, then specify a few more deliverables and a success criterion.

#### 4.5.7 GPU compute as moat (¬ß"Architectural moat implications")

Verbatim:

> GPU compute as foundational architectural capability, combined with field-based gameplay mechanics, creates a structural performance gap vs. CPU-bound competitors in the simulation genre. Steam Hardware Survey median GPU is RTX 3060/4060 class (compute-capable). Major incumbents (RimWorld on Unity, Dwarf Fortress single-thread C++, Prison Architect, Oxygen Not Included, Factorio) are all CPU-bound architecturally and cannot retrofit GPU compute without major engine rewrites.

Per-workload comparison table (verbatim, from ¬ß"Pathfinding scaling ‚Äî secondary moat"):

| Workload | Per-pawn A\* (incumbents) | Flow field (DF) |
|---|---|---|
| 50 pawns, 5 work zones | ~5 ms / tick | ~200 ¬µs / tick |
| 500 pawns, 5 work zones | ~50 ms / tick (unfeasible) | ~250 ¬µs / tick |
| 5000 pawns, 5 work zones | structurally impossible | ~400 ¬µs / tick |

#### 4.5.8 Map-scale assumption

`GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 references `200√ó200` in multiple places: example field dimensions (`RegisterField<float>("vanilla.magic.mana", 200, 200)`), distance field convergence claim ("5‚Äì10 sweep passes converge for 200√ó200 grid"), worst-case memory ("200√ó200 float field = 160 KB per buffer √ó2"), and the ¬ß"Open considerations / Multiple flow fields compete for GPU memory" estimate ("Larger map (1000√ó1000) √ó 32 fields = 256 MB. Still acceptable on modern GPUs").

**Recon finding:** `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 **does not commit to a fixed map size architecturally**. It quotes `200√ó200` as the current illustrative scale and `1000√ó1000` as future scale. The architectural assumption is "modern GPU handles either" ‚Äî i.e. the LOCKED doc is map-size-agnostic at the GPU compute level. The 200√ó200 hardcoding is **lower in the stack** ‚Äî in `NavGrid` allocation at bootstrap (`GameBootstrap.cs:45‚Äì46`) and in `MovementSystem`'s wander-target generation (`MovementSystem.cs:31‚Äì32`). See ¬ß5 for the on-disk evidence.

### 4.6 FIELDS.md v0.1 Live

**Status:** Live (not LOCKED). Populated by K9 closure 2026-05-10. Save/load section reads `TBD until persistence-integration milestone`. The two-system separation principle from FIELDS.md ¬ß"Why two systems, not one" is the architectural distinction recon respects in this report: ECS = sparse, entity-keyed; Fields = dense, cell-keyed. Navigation today lives in ECS (NavGrid is a managed-class peer, not a field); the G6‚ÄìG9 skeleton briefs commit to moving the distance/direction substrate **into** the Field abstraction.

**Field shape (verbatim from ¬ß"Field ‚Äî the unit of storage"):**

> A field is a typed dense 2D array bound to a registered string id. Every field has the same structural shape regardless of element type:
>
> - A primary buffer (`width √ó height √ó sizeof(T)` bytes) holding the current cell values.
> - A back buffer of identical layout used for ping-pong updates by compute kernels (CPU or GPU) that read from one buffer and write to the other.
> - A conductivity map (`width √ó height √ó sizeof(float)` bytes) carrying per-cell diffusion coefficients. Wires, pipes, walls, insulators all express themselves through this map.
> - A storage flag bitmap (`width √ó height` bits, byte-aligned) marking cells that retain capacitance state across ticks.

**Element type T constrained to `unmanaged`.** Common types: `float`, `Vector2`, `int32`. `Vector2`-typed `RawTileField` is the carrier for a flow-field direction layer (G6 skeleton ‚Äî "Direction field compute shader (gradient extraction)"); requires K9 extension for `Vector2` support per the G6 brief's prerequisite list.

### 4.7 FHE_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md v1.0 (precedent for ratified-but-dormant)

**Status:** LOCKED v1.0 (2026-05-06). RATIFIED. Activation pending ¬ßD1 conditions. Dormant period unbounded.

**Recon reads this document as a precedent**: how does the project ratify an architectural capability whose implementation comes years later? Key features:

- **¬ßD1 ‚Äî three independent activation conditions** (production library exists with overhead ‚â§100√ó; stable C#/P-Invoke surface; ‚â•1 shipped game has demonstrated practical integration). Activation gates the entire contract.
- **¬ßD2‚Äì¬ßD8 ‚Äî eight binding decisions** that survive dormancy. Determinism preservation (D2), structural boundary (D3), minimum operation set not maximum (D4), no silent plaintext fallback (D5), capability-system integration (D6), library-version pinning (D7), export compliance noted (D8).
- **¬ß3 ‚Äî empty interface form.** `IHomomorphicComputeProvider` is reserved in `DualFrontier.Crypto.Future` namespace, with no method signatures at v1.0 ‚Äî deliberately. The doc commits the namespace, names the participant types, reserves the binding surface ‚Äî without committing to any specific library's API shape.

**Recon-relevant pattern:** This contract is the project's existing precedent for "ratify the principle while implementation is unknowable; bind specifics when the world is known." A future deliberation on navigation could borrow this shape ‚Äî but recon does not propose this; recon records the precedent's existence.

---

## Section 5 ‚Äî Navigation deep-dive (analytical heart)

Navigation is ‚Äî per the brief ‚Äî the most consequential architectural question on the post-cutover horizon. Three forces converge: (a) dynamic map expansion is the only `IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` item in active backlog; (b) hybrid pathfinding (ad-hoc CPU A\* for ordinary pawns, GPU compute for declared mass events, possibly terrain-shader fields for emergent movement) is the architectural intent recorded in `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 ¬ß"Flow field pathfinding"; (c) the current implementation is a 2026-05 ad-hoc A\* against a fixed `200√ó200` grid in `MovementSystem`. This section quotes the code, then the G-skeleton intent, then maps the gap.

### 5A ‚Äî Current implementation on disk

#### 5A.1 `NavGrid` ‚Äî the grid itself

`src/DualFrontier.AI/Pathfinding/NavGrid.cs` (44 lines, sub-agent transcript). Constructor (lines 18‚Äì27, verbatim):

```csharp
public NavGrid(int width, int height)
{
    Width  = width;
    Height = height;
    _passable = new bool[width * height];
    _cost     = new byte[width * height];
    // default: all passable, cost 1
    Array.Fill(_passable, true);
    Array.Fill(_cost, (byte)1);
}
```

Public surface (per sub-agent extraction):

- `int Width { get; }` (line 12), `int Height { get; }` (line 13) ‚Äî read-only after construction
- `bool IsPassable(int x, int y)` (lines 29‚Äì30)
- `byte GetCost(int x, int y)` (lines 32‚Äì33)
- `void SetTile(int x, int y, bool passable, byte cost = 1)` (lines 35‚Äì40)
- `bool InBounds(int x, int y)` (lines 42‚Äì43)

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **`NavGrid` itself is parametric in `(width, height)`** ‚Äî it does *not* hardcode `200√ó200`. The grid is allocation-only at bootstrap; resize is not exposed.
- **Storage model:** flat `bool[width*height]` for passability + flat `byte[width*height]` for cost. Row-major (implicit `Y * width + X` from `GridVector.ToIndex(int width)` per `GridVector.cs:69`).
- **No tile metadata beyond `(passable, cost)`** ‚Äî no terrain type, no surface type, no per-tile flag for modder extension. A terrain-type system (different walk speeds, different surface costs per terrain class) would require either a parallel byte array or a refactor to a `Tile struct`.
- **No event surface for tile changes** ‚Äî `SetTile` is a write, but nothing observes it. If a tile becomes impassable mid-game (a building constructed), an existing computed path doesn't get invalidated.

#### 5A.2 `AStarPathfinding` ‚Äî the algorithm

`src/DualFrontier.AI/Pathfinding/AStarPathfinding.cs` (92 lines). Constructor + key constant (verbatim):

```csharp
private const int MaxIterations = 2000;

public AStarPathfinding(NavGrid grid)
    => _grid = grid ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(grid));
```

Public surface:

- `bool TryFindPath(GridVector from, GridVector to, out IReadOnlyList<GridVector> path)` (lines 21‚Äì67)
- Private `IEnumerable<GridVector> Neighbors(GridVector v)` (lines 69‚Äì76) ‚Äî 4-directional (N/S/E/W only)
- Private `static float Heuristic(GridVector a, GridVector b)` (lines 78‚Äì79) ‚Äî Manhattan distance
- Private `static void ReconstructPath(...)` (lines 81‚Äì92)

Iteration cap behavior (sub-agent extract):

> Uses `PriorityQueue<GridVector, float>` (line 30) from System.Collections.Generic. Iteration cap enforced at line 38: `while (open.Count > 0 && iterations++ < MaxIterations)`. Returns `false` if MaxIterations exceeded (line 65‚Äì66).

Cost model (sub-agent extract):

> Cost-weighted A*: `tentative = gScore[current] + _grid.GetCost(neighbor.X, neighbor.Y)` (lines 52‚Äì53). 4-directional movement only (no diagonals): N, S, E, W checks at lines 72‚Äì75.

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **`MaxIterations = 2000` is a hard ceiling.** A worst-case path on a `200√ó200` grid with `800` obstacles can hit it (the grid has `40 000` cells; a path along a diagonal could traverse 400+ cells; the A\* open set explores roughly `path_length¬≤` cells in the worst case under Manhattan heuristic). When hit, `TryFindPath` returns `false` and `MovementSystem` simply retries next tick ‚Äî there is no fallback strategy and no diagnostic.
- **4-directional movement, no diagonals.** Chebyshev distance is defined on `GridVector` (¬ß5A.4) but A\* doesn't use it. Diagonal moves are not allowed.
- **No hierarchical pathfinding, no caching.** The README (`src/DualFrontier.AI/Pathfinding/README.md`) records both as roadmap TODOs (per sub-agent extract): "Add hierarchical pathfinding for distant goals" and "Path cache between frequently used pairs of points (invalidation on BuildingPlacedEvent / TileChangedEvent, see PERFORMANCE)."
- **Synchronous-only.** README (verbatim from sub-agent): "Strictly synchronous: no `async/await`, otherwise the `ThreadLocal` system isolation guard breaks." Note: this isolation guard was removed in A'.5 closure (see ¬ß6 below) ‚Äî the synchronous constraint may now be relaxable, but the comment hasn't been updated.

#### 5A.3 `IPathfindingService` ‚Äî the abstraction

`src/DualFrontier.AI/Pathfinding/IPathfindingService.cs` (21 lines, verbatim):

```csharp
public interface IPathfindingService
{
    bool TryFindPath(GridVector from, GridVector to, out IReadOnlyList<GridVector> path);
}
```

A single method. **No async variant. No batch variant. No multi-target variant. No spatial-query variant.** The abstraction surface is exactly one synchronous path-query function ‚Äî `MovementSystem` depends on it through DI; `AStarPathfinding` is the only implementation.

#### 5A.4 `GridVector` ‚Äî the coordinate primitive

`src/DualFrontier.Contracts/Math/GridVector.cs` (93 lines). Type definition (line 6, verbatim):

```csharp
public readonly record struct GridVector(int X, int Y)
```

Public surface (sub-agent extract):

```csharp
public static readonly GridVector Zero  = new(0, 0);
public static readonly GridVector One   = new(1, 1);
public static readonly GridVector Up    = new(0, -1);
public static readonly GridVector Down  = new(0,  1);
public static readonly GridVector Left  = new(-1, 0);
public static readonly GridVector Right = new(1,  0);

public static GridVector operator +(GridVector a, GridVector b);
public static GridVector operator -(GridVector a, GridVector b);
public static GridVector operator *(GridVector a, int scalar);

public int ManhattanDistance(GridVector other);
public int ChebyshevDistance(GridVector other);
public int ToIndex(int width);          // row-major: Y * width + X
public static GridVector FromIndex(int index, int width);
public bool IsInBounds(int width, int height);   // X >= 0 && Y >= 0 && X < width && Y < height
```

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **`X` and `Y` are signed `int`.** The grid supports negative coordinates as type-valid values, but `IsInBounds` checks against `width` and `height` ‚Äî meaning negative coordinates are out of bounds today. The signedness preserves headroom for a future centered-origin coordinate system, but no code currently exploits it.
- **`ToIndex(int width)` is row-major.** Consistent with `NavGrid`'s flat-array layout and `SpatialGrid`'s flat-array layout (`src/DualFrontier.Core/Math/SpatialGrid.cs`, sub-agent extract).
- **No `Vector2`-equivalent float coordinate type exists.** Movement is tile-quantized. A future flow-field-driven movement system (per G6/G7 skeletons) consumes `Vector2`-typed gradients to drive continuous-velocity movement ‚Äî `VelocityComponent` would carry a `Vector2`, and `PositionComponent.Position` (a `GridVector`) would step when the integrated velocity crosses a tile boundary. None of this is in code today.

#### 5A.5 `MovementComponent` and `PositionComponent` ‚Äî the data layer

`src/DualFrontier.Components/Pawn/MovementComponent.cs` (44 lines, verbatim sub-agent extract):

```csharp
[ModAccessible(Read = true, Write = true)]
public struct MovementComponent : IComponent
{
    public GridVector Target;
    public bool HasTarget;
    public NativeComposite<GridVector> Path;
    public int PathStepIndex;
    public int StepCooldown;
}
```

Component-shape commentary (sub-agent extract, lines 25‚Äì35):

> Pathfinding waypoints to Target. Walked sequentially via PathStepIndex rather than via RemoveAt because NativeComposite<GridVector>.RemoveAt uses swap-with-last semantics that would break the FIFO walk order. Path is fully populated at pathfinding time; PathStepIndex advances from 0 toward CountFor(entity); when index == count the path is done.
>
> Default is the invalid sentinel (IsValid == false) ‚Äî must be constructed via NativeWorld.CreateComposite<GridVector>() at factory time before MovementSystem can populate steps.

`src/DualFrontier.Components/Shared/PositionComponent.cs` (16 lines, verbatim):

```csharp
[ModAccessible(Read = true, Write = true)]
public struct PositionComponent : IComponent
{
    /// <summary>Tile-grid coordinate.</summary>
    public GridVector Position;
}
```

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **`Path` is a `NativeComposite<GridVector>` per K8.1 primitives.** Each pawn's path lives in a kernel-side variable-length list, walked sequentially via index (no removal ‚Äî `swap-with-last` would break FIFO). Path memory is owned natively; the managed side holds an opaque handle.
- **No path version, no path generation tag.** When the path is regenerated (e.g. after a tile becomes impassable), the composite is `ClearFor(entity)`-ed and repopulated; there is no version field that downstream readers (e.g. UI displaying a pawn's path) could compare against.
- **`StepCooldown` is a per-pawn integer; no per-terrain speed modifier.** Movement cadence is uniform across terrain (see ¬ß5A.6).

#### 5A.6 `MovementSystem` ‚Äî the consumer

`src/DualFrontier.Systems/Pawn/MovementSystem.cs` (210 lines). Constants at lines 30‚Äì32 (verbatim):

```csharp
private const int StepCooldownTicks = 3;
private const int MapWidth  = 200;
private const int MapHeight = 200;
```

`[SystemAccess]` attribute (lines 22‚Äì27, verbatim):

```csharp
[SystemAccess(
    reads:  new Type[0],
    writes: new[] { typeof(PositionComponent), typeof(MovementComponent) },
    bus:    nameof(IGameServices.Pawns)
)]
[TickRate(TickRates.NORMAL)]
```

The `Update` body is the longest single piece of navigation logic on disk. Quoting the wander-target lookup (lines 143‚Äì149, sub-agent extract ‚Äî verbatim):

```csharp
else
{
    target = new GridVector(
        _rng.Next(0, MapWidth),
        _rng.Next(0, MapHeight));
    isExternalTarget = false;
}
```

And the path-population step (lines 156‚Äì161, verbatim):

```csharp
if (!move.Path.IsValid)
    move.Path = NativeWorld.CreateComposite<GridVector>();
else
    move.Path.ClearFor(entity);
foreach (GridVector step in path)
    move.Path.Add(entity, step);
move.PathStepIndex = 0;
```

And the per-tick step (lines 174‚Äì177, verbatim):

```csharp
move.Path.TryGetAt(entity, move.PathStepIndex, out GridVector next);
move.PathStepIndex++;
pos.Position = next;
move.StepCooldown = StepCooldownTicks;
```

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **The `200, 200` constants at lines 31‚Äì32 are duplicates of `GameBootstrap.cs:45‚Äì46` (next subsection).** A change to map size must be reflected in both files. There is no single configuration source.
- **The wander logic (lines 145‚Äì147) uses `_rng.Next(0, MapWidth)` and `_rng.Next(0, MapHeight)`** ‚Äî i.e. it generates targets in the half-open range `[0, MapWidth)`. This is upper-bound-exclusive, which matches `GridVector.IsInBounds`'s `X < width && Y < height` convention. Consistent today; brittle if either side flips convention.
- **Movement cadence is `1 tile per 4 ticks`** ‚Äî set `StepCooldown = 3`, decrement each tick, advance when reaches 0. Tick rate is `TickRates.NORMAL`. The README (¬ß3 Phase 3) reads "A* pathfinding (2000-iteration cap, no cache)" ‚Äî confirming both the iteration cap (see ¬ß5A.2) and the absence of caching.
- **Two separate `WriteBatch` scopes** (post-cutover) ‚Äî one for `PositionComponent`, one for `MovementComponent`. The comment on lines 96‚Äì101 (sub-agent extract): "Snapshot the MovementComponent span and per-entity PositionComponent reads, then apply (Position, Movement) writes via two batches once the lease is released. Two BeginBatch scopes are needed because each batch is single-type."
- **One `SpanLease<MovementComponent>` acquisition per tick** (lines 102‚Äì112, sub-agent extract) ‚Äî the entire system iterates over all moving entities in a single snapshot.

#### 5A.7 `GameBootstrap` ‚Äî where the navigation system is wired

`src/DualFrontier.Application/Loop/GameBootstrap.cs` (200 lines). Constants block at lines 45‚Äì55 (verbatim sub-agent extract):

```csharp
private const int MapWidth = 200;
private const int MapHeight = 200;
private const int InitialPawnCount = 50;
private const int FactorySeed = 42;
private const int ObstacleSeed = 42;
private const int ObstacleCount = 800;
private const int InitialFoodCount       = 150;
private const int InitialWaterCount      = 50;
private const int InitialBedCount        = 30;
private const int InitialDecorationCount = 25;
private const int ItemFactorySeed        = 43;
```

NavGrid allocation + obstacle population at lines 82‚Äì90 (verbatim):

```csharp
var navGrid = new NavGrid(MapWidth, MapHeight);
var obstacleRng = new Random(ObstacleSeed);
for (int i = 0; i < ObstacleCount; i++)
{
    int ox = obstacleRng.Next(0, MapWidth);
    int oy = obstacleRng.Next(0, MapHeight);
    navGrid.SetTile(ox, oy, passable: false);
}
var pathfinding = new AStarPathfinding(navGrid);
```

MovementSystem injection at line 126 (verbatim):

```csharp
new MovementSystem(pathfinding),
```

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **All scale assumptions live in this file.** `200√ó200` map, `50` pawns, `800` obstacles, `150` food / `50` water / `30` beds / `25` decorations. Seeds `42` and `43` are RNG anchors.
- **`NavGrid` is allocation-only.** Once `new NavGrid(MapWidth, MapHeight)` runs, the grid is fixed-size for the rest of the session. No resize API exists. Dynamic map expansion (per IDEAS_RESERVOIR ¬ß1) is incompatible with the current allocation shape.
- **The obstacle pattern is purely random.** No clustering, no structure, no biome variation. Real terrain would come from a `MapSystem` consuming a generated terrain dataset ‚Äî `MapSystem.cs` exists as a Phase-7 stub (per ¬ß10), no implementation yet.
- **`AStarPathfinding(navGrid)` takes the NavGrid in ctor.** Replacing the pathfinder means re-constructing this object ‚Äî there's no hot-swap, and `MovementSystem` holds the `IPathfindingService` reference indefinitely.

#### 5A.8 Hardcoded scale assumptions ‚Äî consolidated table

| File | Line | Constant | Value | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 45 | `MapWidth` | `200` | Used by `new NavGrid(MapWidth, MapHeight)` |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 46 | `MapHeight` | `200` | Used by `new NavGrid(MapWidth, MapHeight)` |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 47 | `InitialPawnCount` | `50` | `pawnFactory.Spawn()` arg |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 48 | `FactorySeed` | `42` | RNG anchor |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 49 | `ObstacleSeed` | `42` | RNG anchor |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 50 | `ObstacleCount` | `800` | impassable tiles seeded at bootstrap |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 51 | `InitialFoodCount` | `150` | factory seed count |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 52 | `InitialWaterCount` | `50` | factory seed count |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 53 | `InitialBedCount` | `30` | factory seed count |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 54 | `InitialDecorationCount` | `25` | factory seed count |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 55 | `ItemFactorySeed` | `43` | RNG anchor |
| `MovementSystem.cs` | 30 | `StepCooldownTicks` | `3` | per-pawn step cadence |
| `MovementSystem.cs` | 31 | `MapWidth` | `200` | **duplicate** of `GameBootstrap.cs:45` |
| `MovementSystem.cs` | 32 | `MapHeight` | `200` | **duplicate** of `GameBootstrap.cs:46` |
| `AStarPathfinding.cs` | 14 | `MaxIterations` | `2000` | A* per-query iteration cap |
| `GameRoot.cs` | 20 | `MapWidth` | `200` | TileMap initialization (Presentation layer) |
| `GameRoot.cs` | 21 | `MapHeight` | `200` | TileMap initialization (Presentation layer) |
| `IsotropicDiffusionKernel.cs` | 20 (string) | ‚Äî | `"200√ó200"` | comment: "40 000 P/Invokes per iteration on a 200√ó200 field" |
| `GameRoot.cs` | 59 (string) | ‚Äî | `"200√ó200√ó16"` | comment about pixel size of map |
| `TileMapRenderer.cs` | 23 | `DefaultSeed` | `42` | RNG anchor (Presentation) |

**The `200` value is hardcoded in at least three independent source-of-truth locations** (`GameBootstrap.cs`, `MovementSystem.cs`, `GameRoot.cs`), plus appears in two diagnostic comments. Any change to map size must be reflected in all three. **There is no central `IWorldDimensions` / `MapConfig` / `IGameConfiguration` surface that any of these would consult.** This is a Layer A finding of high severity for the dynamic-map-expansion question.

### 5B ‚Äî G-series skeleton intent (G6‚ÄìG9, navigation-specific)

The G-series skeletons are the project's authored forward intent for navigation. Each was sub-agent-read in full and is summarized below with verbatim Goal/Deliverables/Success-criteria. The complete skeleton-vs-authored maturity comparison is in ¬ß9.

#### 5B.1 G6 ‚Äî Flow field infrastructure

Path: `tools/briefs/G6_FLOW_FIELD_INFRASTRUCTURE_BRIEF.md` (41 lines, 1 356 bytes, AUTHORED + Status: NOT STARTED). Prerequisite: G5 closed (or G4 if scheduling permits early start).

**Goal (verbatim):** "Engine-side flow field primitives. No gameplay binding yet. Distance field compute shader (Option B simple diffusion variant initially); direction field compute shader (gradient extraction); `Vector2` field type support –≤ kernel (K9 extension)."

**Deliverables (sub-agent extract):** distance field compute shader (Option B); direction field compute shader (gradient extraction); `Vector2` field type kernel support (K9 extension required).

**Success criterion (verbatim):** "Distance field converges; gradient points correctly toward target on representative grids."

**Recon notes:**
- G6 commits to **Option B (simple diffusion)** as the initial distance-field math, per `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 ¬ß"Distance field math". Option A (Eikonal FSM/FMM) is deferred to G9, gated on measurement.
- **K9 extension required** ‚Äî current K9 field storage supports `unmanaged T` cell types, and `Vector2` is `unmanaged`; the "extension" is likely the typed managed-side wrapper, not a native-side change. (No code verifies this; the brief's prerequisite is the only signal.)
- G6 is **engine-side**, no gameplay binding. Consistent with the "engine provides infrastructure" line in `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0.

#### 5B.2 G7 ‚Äî `Vanilla.Movement` integration

Path: `tools/briefs/G7_VANILLA_MOVEMENT_BRIEF.md` (43 lines, 1 470 bytes, AUTHORED + Status: NOT STARTED). Prerequisite: G6 closed.

**Goal (verbatim):** "Pathfinding ships through a vanilla mod, replacing per-pawn A* on common destinations. Flow field lifecycle management (creation, refresh, eviction); per-target field assignment logic; movement system reads direction field, applies –∫ velocity; hybrid A* fallback –¥–ª—è unique destinations (preserves `PathfindingService` –¥–ª—è residual case)."

**Deliverables (sub-agent extract):** flow field lifecycle management (LRU eviction, max 32 active); per-target field assignment logic; movement system reads direction field, applies to velocity; hybrid A\* fallback wiring; `Vanilla.Movement` mod.

**Success criterion (verbatim):** "50+ pawns navigating shared work zones consume O(K) field updates regardless of pawn count."

**Recon notes:**
- G7 is **the load-bearing milestone for the hybrid pathfinding architecture** (`GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 ¬ß"When flow field wins, when A* still needed" ‚Äî flow field default, A\* fallback).
- **`PathfindingService` is explicitly preserved** for the residual case. This is the existing `IPathfindingService` / `AStarPathfinding` pair from ¬ß5A.2 ‚Äî the recon-current code is not deleted by G7, it is downgraded from "primary navigation" to "residual A\* fallback".
- **Max 32 active flow fields** ‚Äî see ¬ß5C.3 for the memory-budget implication.
- **Movement system reads direction field, applies to velocity** ‚Äî this implies introducing a `VelocityComponent`-like notion (or extending `MovementComponent`). `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0's worked example shows `WriteCommandBuffer.Update(... new VelocityComponent { Vector = direction * intent.Speed })` ‚Äî `VelocityComponent` is not a current component; it would be introduced by `Vanilla.Movement`.
- **Field lifecycle: created on first request, evicted via LRU, max 32 active.** Per-target ephemeral resources. The lifecycle is **mod-managed**, not engine-managed.

#### 5B.3 G8 ‚Äî Local avoidance layer

Path: `tools/briefs/G8_LOCAL_AVOIDANCE_BRIEF.md` (41 lines, 1 182 bytes, AUTHORED + Status: NOT STARTED). Prerequisite: G7 closed.

**Goal (verbatim):** "Local steering on top of flow field direction. RVO-like or simple boids approach; combines flow field global direction + local agent collision avoidance. Pure managed CPU code (per-pawn, –Ω–æ simple math, parallelizable)."

**Deliverables (sub-agent extract):** RVO or boids local steering layer; combines with flow field gradient; managed CPU implementation; per-pawn cost benchmark.

**Success criterion (verbatim):** "Pawns following identical gradient do not cluster / jam / oscillate beyond tolerance."

**Recon notes:**
- G8 is **purely managed CPU code**. Per-pawn cost is the budget constraint. Per `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 ¬ß"Local avoidance is not free": "Cost: per-pawn local steering (~100 ¬µs for 200 pawns). Still much cheaper than per-pawn A*."
- **RVO or boids ‚Äî choice left open.** A G8 authored brief would have to lock the algorithm; the skeleton does not.
- **The "ad-hoc CPU paths" half of the user's hybrid intent** lives here architecturally. G8 is the lane where per-pawn fine-grained navigation work happens; the flow field (G6/G7) handles the bulk steering vector.

#### 5B.4 G9 ‚Äî Eikonal upgrade (optional, evidence-gated)

Path: `tools/briefs/G9_EIKONAL_UPGRADE_BRIEF.md` (40 lines, 1 345 bytes, AUTHORED + Status: NOT STARTED, **conditional**). Prerequisite: G7 closed + measurement evidence.

**Goal (verbatim):** "Replace simple diffusion —Å Fast Sweeping Method (FSM) –∏–ª–∏ Fast Marching Method (FMM) when measurement justifies it. Geodesic-accurate distances; reuse direction field extraction unchanged. Side-by-side comparison —Å Option B output on shipped scenarios."

**Success criterion (verbatim):** "Only shipped if Option B measurement shows gameplay-relevant suboptimality. Otherwise the milestone closes –±–µ–∑ code, —Å measurement archived."

**Recon notes:**
- **G9 is the project's first evidence-gated milestone** ‚Äî its closure shape includes "closes without code if Option B is sufficient". The brief itself records this; the design intent is "ratify the principle of optional upgrade; defer activation to measurement."
- **Borrows the FHE contract precedent** (¬ß4.7) in structure though not in form: "implementation deferred pending evidence" rather than "implementation deferred pending technology". G9 is the analog at the navigation layer.

### 5C ‚Äî Code-vs-skeleton interlock map

#### 5C.1 What is on-disk vs what each G-skeleton commits to

| G-skeleton | Architectural intent | Code today | Drift / gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| G6 (flow field infra) | Engine-side distance + direction field compute shaders; `Vector2` field type | No flow field code; `Vector2`-typed field not exercised; CPU `IsotropicDiffusionKernel` reference exists but no flow-field-specific kernel | Pure forward delta. K9 + G0 prerequisites partly met (K9 done; G0 not). |
| G7 (`Vanilla.Movement`) | Flow field lifecycle in a mod; movement system reads direction field; A\* preserved as fallback | `MovementSystem` does per-pawn A\* in the engine, not a mod; no `Vanilla.Movement` mod assembly; no `VelocityComponent`; no flow field lifecycle | Largest delta. G7 requires `MovementSystem` to be replaced by a mod-side system; the K-L9 "vanilla = mods" principle is the architectural anchor for this. M8 (vanilla skeletons) per `ROADMAP.md` is "‚è≠ Pending" ‚Äî no vanilla mod assemblies exist yet. |
| G8 (local avoidance) | Managed CPU per-pawn steering combining flow field + collision | `MovementSystem` walks `move.Path` step-by-step with no collision-avoidance logic; pawns can stand on the same tile (no occupancy check) | Pure forward delta. The K-L9 mod placement decision applies ‚Äî G8 likely ships as part of `Vanilla.Movement` or a peer. |
| G9 (Eikonal upgrade) | Conditional FSM/FMM upgrade based on measurement | No flow field at all; question is moot pre-G6 | Pure forward delta; depends on G7 measurement evidence. |

#### 5C.2 The dynamic-map-expansion requirement

`IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` ¬ß1 reads:

> The active surface for current development is [ROADMAP](./ROADMAP.md). The current scope explicitly excludes everything in this document, **with the single exception of dynamic map expansion, which lives in the active backlog**. All other entries are post-release.

Recon searches the active surface (`ROADMAP.md`, `PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING.md`, `MIGRATION_PLAN_KERNEL_TO_VANILLA.md`, `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md`, `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md`, all G-skeletons) for "dynamic map expansion", "map expansion", "map growth", "expandable map", "map resize". **No occurrence found.** The reservoir item is named once, in the reservoir's reading-discipline section, and never operationalized.

What dynamic map expansion would require touching, given the recon's findings:

| Subsystem | Today | What expansion would require |
|---|---|---|
| `NavGrid` allocation | `new NavGrid(MapWidth, MapHeight)` once at bootstrap; `bool[]` + `byte[]` flat arrays | Resize-capable allocation (grow the flat arrays; preserve existing tile state). Or replace with chunked grid (`Dictionary<ChunkKey, NavGridChunk>`). Either way, code change. |
| `AStarPathfinding` | Holds `_grid` ref; iterates 4-neighbor in `Neighbors()`; bounds checks via `_grid.IsPassable` | If `NavGrid` mutates dimensions, iterators stay correct because they go through `_grid.IsPassable`. But path caches (if added later) become invalid on resize. |
| `MovementSystem` | Hardcodes `MapWidth = 200, MapHeight = 200` (lines 31‚Äì32) for wander target generation | The constants must be replaced with a runtime query against the current map size. |
| `GameBootstrap` | Hardcodes `MapWidth = 200, MapHeight = 200` (lines 45‚Äì46) | Must consult a runtime map-size source. Initial bootstrap is fine; mid-game expansion requires a new event/command path. |
| `GameRoot` (Presentation) | Hardcodes `MapWidth = 200, MapHeight = 200` (lines 20‚Äì21) for TileMap initialization | Must observe a map-size-changed event; resize TileMap. |
| `GridVector.IsInBounds` | Takes `(width, height)` parameters | Already parametric ‚Äî no change. |
| `SpatialGrid` (`src/DualFrontier.Core/Math/SpatialGrid.cs`) | Constructor takes `(width, height)`, allocates `HashSet<EntityId>[width * height]` | Must support resize. Same shape problem as `NavGrid`. |
| Flow fields (G6/G7 future) | Per `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0: "Larger map (1000√ó1000) √ó 32 fields = 256 MB. Still acceptable on modern GPUs." | Field allocation is `(width, height)`-parametric; G6 design accepts arbitrary scale. **But** existing field registrations would need to be re-allocated on map resize (or live with the old size). |
| `MapSystem` (Phase 7 stub) | `TODO: Phase 0 ‚Äî determine the area of interest, load/unload regions.` (sub-agent extract from `MapSystem.cs:31`) | The natural home for chunk-management logic, currently empty. |
| Save/load (Persistence) | `TileEncoder` / `ComponentEncoder` per Phase 4 closure | Snapshot serialization assumes fixed dimensions today; expansion requires variable-size snapshots. |

**Recon finding (Layer A, high severity):** Dynamic map expansion intersects at least 9 subsystems with no architectural surface currently committed. The IDEAS_RESERVOIR claim "lives in the active backlog" is not reflected in any active artifact recon could find ‚Äî no brief is authored, no LOCKED doc references it as a requirement, no G-skeleton scopes to handle it. This is a **named but unrepresented** backlog item.

#### 5C.3 The GPU compute boundary for navigation

`GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 commits to GPU compute as foundational architecture. The G-series roadmap (¬ß4.5.6) ships `Fields` (K9, done) then `ComputePipelines` (G0, **not done**). `IModComputePipelineApi` is currently a stub (per sub-agent extract `src/DualFrontier.Contracts/Modding/IModComputePipelineApi.cs:9`):

```csharp
public interface IModComputePipelineApi
{
    string Name { get; }
}
```

And `RestrictedModApi.ComputePipelines => null` in production (per sub-agent extract). G0 is the milestone that wires the real surface.

**The G6/G7 skeletons depend on `ComputePipelines` being non-null.** Without G0, the flow-field shaders cannot be registered; without flow fields, the G7 hybrid architecture has nothing to dispatch. **G0's prerequisite chain is M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 (Vulkan instance/device for rendering) + K9 closure.** K9 is done. M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 are `‚è≠ Pending` per `ROADMAP.md`.

**Recon finding (Layer C, high relevance):** The navigation transformation from "ad-hoc CPU A\*" to "hybrid flow field + A\* fallback" depends on a chain: M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 (Vulkan runtime) ‚Üí G0 (compute pipeline plumbing) ‚Üí G1‚ÄìG5 (field-domain proof) ‚Üí G6 (flow field infra) ‚Üí G7 (`Vanilla.Movement`). All five are skeleton-state. The user's "ad-hoc CPU paths + GPU declared mass events" hybrid is **architecturally specified in `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0** but **operationally five skeleton milestones away from execution**.

#### 5C.4 Mod-extensibility of navigation

`MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß4.6 commits `IModApi` v3 with `Fields` (live) and `ComputePipelines` (stub). The G7 brief commits to `Vanilla.Movement` registering flow field types per target category. The cross-mod access surface is governed by `IModFieldApi.GetField<T>(id)` with `mod.{owner}.field.read:{fieldId}` capability declaration.

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **A mod can register a custom navigation strategy today only by shipping its own pathfinding logic and not depending on `IPathfindingService`.** `IPathfindingService` is `internal`-shaped (it lives in `DualFrontier.AI`, not `DualFrontier.Contracts`) ‚Äî mods cannot replace `IPathfindingService` directly.
- **G7's "vanilla = mods" placement means `Vanilla.Movement` becomes the canonical path mods extend or replace.** Third-party movement strategies would target the same `IModApi.Fields` surface and the same flow-field-vs-A\* hybrid pattern.
- **Today, mods have no path-related capability verb.** The capability set today (`read`, `write`, `publish`, `subscribe`, `field.*`, `pipeline.register`) does not include navigation-specific verbs. A `nav.path.compute` or `nav.field.read` verb may be needed if `Vanilla.Movement` exposes shared path queries.

## Section 6 ‚Äî Scheduler and ECS kernel

The scheduler is `ParallelSystemScheduler`; the systems hang off `SystemBase`; the dependency graph is computed by `DependencyGraph`. Post-A'.5, the runtime isolation guard is removed ‚Äî compile-time `[SystemAccess]` is the sole isolation mechanism, pending the A'.7 Roslyn analyzer. Navigation's place in the schedule is as one of the 10 production systems on the `IGameServices.Pawns` bus, running at `TickRates.NORMAL`.

### 6.1 `ParallelSystemScheduler` (`src/DualFrontier.Core/Scheduling/ParallelSystemScheduler.cs`, 263 lines)

Post-A'.5 constructor (sub-agent extract, lines 70‚Äì103, verbatim):

```csharp
public ParallelSystemScheduler(
    IReadOnlyList<SystemPhase> phases,
    TickScheduler ticks,
    IReadOnlyDictionary<SystemBase, SystemMetadata> systemMetadata,
    IModFaultSink faultSink,
    NativeWorld nativeWorld,
    IGameServices? services = null,
    IManagedStorageResolver? managedStorageResolver = null)
{
    _phases = phases ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(phases));
    _ticks = ticks ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ticks));
    _systemMetadata = systemMetadata ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(systemMetadata));
    _faultSink = faultSink ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(faultSink));
    _nativeWorld = nativeWorld ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(nativeWorld));
    _services = services;
    _managedStorageResolver = managedStorageResolver;
    _parallelOptions = new ParallelOptions
    {
        MaxDegreeOfParallelism = System.Math.Max(1, Environment.ProcessorCount - 2),
    };
    // ... context cache build, InitializeAllSystems
}
```

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **No `World` parameter.** Pre-cutover, the constructor took the managed `World`; post-A'.5 it takes `NativeWorld` only. Path Œ≤ resolution goes through `IManagedStorageResolver`.
- **`MaxDegreeOfParallelism = Environment.ProcessorCount - 2`** (minimum 1) ‚Äî leaves headroom for rendering + main threads. Per-phase parallelism via `Parallel.ForEach`.
- **Execution model (sub-agent extract, line 147):** `Parallel.ForEach(phase.Systems, _parallelOptions, system => { if (!_ticks.ShouldRun(system)) return; ‚Ä¶ system.Update(delta); ‚Ä¶ })`. Systems in the same phase run in parallel; phases run sequentially.

### 6.2 `DependencyGraph` (`src/DualFrontier.Core/Scheduling/DependencyGraph.cs`, 261 lines)

The graph computes phases via topological sort. Sub-agent finding (verbatim, line 86): `var readSet = new HashSet<Type>(); foreach (Type t in access.Reads) readSet.Add(t);`. The graph **operates on `HashSet<Type>` ‚Äî component types, not spatial tiles or entity IDs.**

**Recon-relevant facts (sub-agent's cross-cutting finding Q3):**

- **No tile-level read declaration.** A system declares `reads: [PositionComponent]` ‚Äî the dependency graph treats every position read as overlapping every position write. There is no "this system only reads tile (x, y)"; ergo there is no "only reads tile (x, y) for tile-local navigation".
- **Spatial conflicts are not expressed in the dependency graph.** If two pawns target the same tile, this is a runtime conflict to resolve in event handlers, not a structural conflict the graph prevents.
- **Implication for navigation:** A flow-field-driven system that reads `FieldHandle<Vector2>` (gradient) would be a **field read**, not a component read. The dependency graph as currently shaped doesn't know about field reads ‚Äî they bypass the read-set entirely. This is consistent with the field/ECS separation in `FIELDS.md` v0.1.

### 6.3 `SystemBase` (`src/DualFrontier.Core/ECS/SystemBase.cs`, 129 lines) and `SystemExecutionContext` (162 lines)

Sub-agent extract: post-A'.5, `SystemBase` exposes:

- `protected NativeWorld NativeWorld { get; }` ‚Äî current context's NativeWorld (throws if outside context)
- `protected IGameServices Services { get; }` ‚Äî current context's services
- `protected ManagedStore<T>? ManagedStore<T>() where T : class, IComponent` ‚Äî Path Œ≤ resolver

**`SystemBase.GetComponent` / `SystemBase.SetComponent` / `SystemBase.Query` / `SystemBase.GetSystem` were deleted at A'.5.** Per the K8.34 v2 brief ¬ß0.2: "Œ± hard cutover. No backward compatibility. No legacy API survival. `SystemBase.GetComponent/SetComponent/Query/Query<T1,T2>/GetSystem` deleted. `SystemExecutionContext` refactored: `World` parameter removed, `NativeWorld` required."

### 6.4 `SystemAccessAttribute` (`src/DualFrontier.Contracts/Attributes/SystemAccessAttribute.cs`, 72 lines)

Sub-agent extract (line 17):

```csharp
[AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class, Inherited = false, AllowMultiple = false)]
public sealed class SystemAccessAttribute : Attribute
```

Two constructors:

```csharp
public SystemAccessAttribute(Type[] reads, Type[] writes, string bus);
public SystemAccessAttribute(Type[] reads, Type[] writes, string[] buses);
```

Properties: `Reads: Type[]`, `Writes: Type[]`, `Bus: string`, `Buses: string[]`. The `Inherited = false` flag is the K-L4 enforcement: every system class must declare its access set explicitly.

### 6.5 Post-A'.5 isolation discipline

The K8.34 v2 brief ¬ß0.2 reads: "Isolation guard runtime checks deleted (compile-time `[SystemAccess]` + future A'.9 analyzer)." The runtime guard was a `ThreadLocal<>` mechanism in `SystemExecutionContext` that checked, on every component access, whether the current system was authorized to read/write the type. With it removed, **discipline is enforced only at the schedule-construction layer (via `DependencyGraph`) and at code review.** The A'.9 Roslyn analyzer (per `PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING.md` ¬ß2) is the structural replacement ‚Äî but A'.9 is two milestones away (A'.6 = K8.5 mod-ecosystem prep, A'.7 = analyzer authoring, A'.8 = K-closure report, A'.9 = analyzer execution).

**Recon-relevant fact (Layer A medium severity):** The `MovementSystem`'s `[SystemAccess(reads: new Type[0], writes: ‚Ä¶)]` declares no reads, but the system body reads `PositionComponent` via `NativeWorld.TryGetComponent<PositionComponent>(entity, out PositionComponent pos)` (sub-agent extract of `MovementSystem.Update`, line ~111). This **violates the read declaration** ‚Äî but no analyzer catches it today. The runtime guard would have caught this pre-A'.5. The compile-time analyzer (A'.9, future) is intended to catch it. **Today, the only enforcement is the dependency graph noticing that no system writes `PositionComponent` while `MovementSystem` runs ‚Äî which is incidentally true** (only `MovementSystem` itself and `ProjectileSystem` write `PositionComponent`, and they don't overlap in tick-rate). The invariant holds by coincidence of current schedule shape, not by structural enforcement.

---

## Section 7 ‚Äî Native kernel and compute boundary

### 7.1 Surface inventory

The native kernel exposes ~58 `extern "C"` functions across the C ABI header `native/DualFrontier.Core.Native/include/df_capi.h` (529 lines per sub-agent). Grouped by domain (sub-agent extract):

| Domain | Functions | Status |
|---|---|---|
| World lifecycle | 7 | Stable K0+ |
| Component ops | 5 | Stable K0+ |
| Bulk ops (K1) | 2 | Stable K1 |
| Span leases (K1) | 2 | Stable K1 |
| Type registration (K2) | 1 | Stable K2 |
| Bootstrap (K3) | 1 | Stable K3 |
| Command buffer (K5) | 7 | Stable K5 |
| String pool (K8.1) | 8 | Stable K8.1 |
| Keyed maps (K8.1) | 7 | Stable K8.1 |
| Composites (K8.1) | 7 | Stable K8.1 |
| Sets (K8.1) | 6 | Stable K8.1 |
| Field storage (K9) | 12 | Stable K9 |
| **GPU compute** | **0** | **NOT IMPLEMENTED** |

The managed bridge `NativeWorld.cs` (695 lines, sub-agent extract) wraps these as typed C# methods. Key entry points the cutover exposed are `AcquireSpan<T>`, `BeginBatch<T>`, `CreateMap`/`CreateSet`/`CreateComposite`, `InternString`, `AllocateMapId`/`AllocateSetId`/`AllocateCompositeId`, plus `Fields` (the `FieldRegistry` accessor introduced at K9).

### 7.2 Field storage primitives (K9)

`FieldHandle<T>` (`src/DualFrontier.Core.Interop/FieldHandle.cs`, 250 lines) is the managed-side surface to a registered field. Sub-agent extract ‚Äî key methods:

```csharp
public sealed class FieldHandle<T> : IFieldHandle where T : unmanaged
{
    public string Id => _fieldId;
    public int Width => _width;
    public int Height => _height;
    public Type ElementType => typeof(T);

    public unsafe T ReadCell(int x, int y);
    public unsafe void WriteCell(int x, int y, T value);
    public unsafe FieldSpanLease<T> AcquireSpan();
    public unsafe void SetConductivity(int x, int y, float value);
    public unsafe float GetConductivity(int x, int y);
    public unsafe void SetStorageFlag(int x, int y, bool enabled);
    public unsafe bool GetStorageFlag(int x, int y);
    public unsafe void SwapBuffers();
}
```

Plus `FieldSpanLease<T>` (an unsafe ref struct that wraps a pinned span over native field memory).

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **Field width/height are mod-controlled per `Register<T>(string id, int width, int height)`.** No engine-side limit; per-cell memory is `Width √ó Height √ó sizeof(T)`.
- **The compute-back-buffer is allocated at field registration** (per `FIELDS.md` v0.1: "The back buffer is allocated at field registration time, not lazily on first dispatch ‚Äî there is no ¬´field without back buffer¬ª state"). `SwapBuffers()` is the API; on the CPU path it swaps managed buffer pointers; on a future GPU path it would be the host-side analog of fence-based readback.

### 7.3 GPU compute boundary ‚Äî the gap

The compute-pipeline surface is reserved but stub-shaped. Sub-agent extract (`src/DualFrontier.Contracts/Modding/IModComputePipelineApi.cs`, full file):

```csharp
namespace DualFrontier.Contracts.Modding;

/// <summary>
/// Compute-pipeline sub-API placeholder per <c>MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md</c>
/// v1.7 ¬ß4.6. Ships at G0 alongside Vulkan compute plumbing; on K9-only
/// builds <see cref="IModApi.ComputePipelines"/> returns <c>null</c> and
/// mods degrade gracefully.
/// </summary>
public interface IModComputePipelineApi
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Identifier for the implementation. K9 ships no implementation;
    /// G0 wires the real surface.
    /// </summary>
    string Name { get; }
}
```

`RestrictedModApi.cs` (sub-agent extract):

```csharp
public IModComputePipelineApi? ComputePipelines => null;
```

The full `IModComputePipelineApi` surface specified in `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß4.6.1 (verbatim from ¬ß4.2 above) commits to:

```csharp
public interface IModComputePipelineApi
{
    ComputePipelineHandle RegisterPipeline(string id, byte[] spirvBytes);
    ComputePipelineHandle GetPipeline(string id);
}
```

The on-disk interface (1 property, `Name`) is **much narrower** than the LOCKED-doc spec (2 methods, `RegisterPipeline` + `GetPipeline`). This is consistent with the placeholder framing ‚Äî the interface is reserved for G0 to expand.

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **The G-series skeleton briefs (G0‚ÄìG9) consume a compute-pipeline interface that does not exist in code yet.** This is not a fault ‚Äî the briefs are forward intent for a milestone that hasn't been authored ‚Äî but it is a fact: the G-skeletons cannot be authored-shape into execution-ready briefs against the current `IModComputePipelineApi` because the surface they prescribe (`RegisterPipeline(id, spirvBytes)`) is not declared.
- **The K9 field-back-buffer layout is forward-compatible.** Per the C ABI header (sub-agent extract, lines 427‚Äì428): `* - Back buffer: identical layout (ping-pong target for compute kernels)`. The back buffer is allocated; the swap function exists; what's missing is the GPU-side binding of the buffer + the SPIR-V upload path + the fence-based dispatch API.
- **No Vulkan headers, no compute shader infrastructure, no `glslangValidator` invocations in the build.** The G0 milestone is fundamentally a build-pipeline + native-side Vulkan-compute-context addition. The K9 work proved the field-storage shape; G0 proves the dispatch shape.

### 7.4 Direction-discipline (K-L7) and what compute would need

`KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` Part 0 / `GPU_COMPUTE.md` ¬ß"Direction-discipline (KERNEL K-L7)":

> Managed ‚Üí native always. Field reads and writes flow through the managed bridge to native C ABI. Native kernel issues GPU compute to `vulkan-1.dll`. No reverse P/Invoke. No GPU-to-managed callbacks. No managed code in the GPU execution path.

So G0 adds **native-side Vulkan compute plumbing**, not managed-side. From the C# perspective, the surface is `FieldHandle<T>.DispatchCompute(ComputePipelineHandle, ReadOnlySpan<byte> pushConstants, int iterations)` per `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß4.6.1. This managed-side method becomes a single P/Invoke `df_world_field_dispatch_compute` to native, which records the command buffer and submits to the compute queue.

**The K-L7 direction-discipline shapes the entire navigation transformation:** flow-field math runs natively (or on the GPU); the managed `MovementSystem` (or its replacement `Vanilla.Movement`) reads the resulting direction field via P/Invoke per pawn per tick. The per-pawn cost is one P/Invoke per pawn per tick to read the gradient cell ‚Äî `200 pawns √ó 1 P/Invoke ‚âà trivial` (cited in `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0).

---

## Section 8 ‚Äî Mod API v3 surface

`IModApi` v3 is the surface every mod sees. The full surface lives in `src/DualFrontier.Contracts/Modding/IModApi.cs` and is specified in `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` v1.7 ¬ß4.6.1. Recon's quoted version is in ¬ß4.2 of this report. Three observations specific to recon's questions:

### 8.1 Two-axis registration model

- **Path Œ± ‚Äî `RegisterComponent<T> where T : IComponent`** (v2 surface, preserved). Path Œ± is the default; the type must be a struct that conforms to `IComponent`. Storage lives in `NativeWorld` (K-L11).
- **Path Œ≤ ‚Äî `RegisterManagedComponent<T> where T : class, IComponent`** (v3 addition). Type must be a class with `[ManagedStorage]` attribute. Storage lives per-mod in `RestrictedModApi`'s `ManagedStore<T>`, reclaimed on `AssemblyLoadContext.Unload`. The `MissingManagedStorageAttribute` validation error (per `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß11.2) catches shape/attribute mismatches at load time.

### 8.2 Two sub-API slots

- **`Fields { get; }` ‚Äî `IModFieldApi?`** ‚Äî K9 enables; null on pre-K9 builds. Mod startup pattern (per `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß4.6.4): check for null, degrade.
- **`ComputePipelines { get; }` ‚Äî `IModComputePipelineApi?`** ‚Äî G0 enables; null today. The current placeholder surface (1 property) is narrower than the spec'd surface (2 methods) ‚Äî see ¬ß7.3.

### 8.3 Mod API v3 backward compatibility

Per `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß4.6.3:

> A v2 mod that does not reference `Fields` or `ComputePipelines` continues to compile and load against v3 unchanged. The v3 properties default to null on builds that do not include K9/G-series support; v2 mods never observe them. v1 mods (no-op `Publish`/`Subscribe`) continue under the ¬ß4.5 grace period.

**Recon-relevant fact:** The Mod API has an established "additive new surface, null-degraded on old builds" pattern. The G7 (`Vanilla.Movement`) deliverable extends this pattern: the navigation surface is mod-extensible without breaking the contract.

### 8.4 What is reserved vs what is live

| Surface | Status | Mod can use today? |
|---|---|---|
| `RegisterComponent<T>` (Path Œ±) | Live (v2) | Yes |
| `RegisterManagedComponent<T>` (Path Œ≤) | Live (v3) | Yes (post-K-L3.1, post-A'.5) |
| `RegisterSystem`, `Publish`, `Subscribe` | Live (v2) | Yes |
| `PublishContract` / `TryGetContract` | Live (v2) | Yes |
| `GetKernelCapabilities` / `GetOwnManifest` / `Log` | Live (v2) | Yes |
| `Fields` (sub-API) | Live (v3, K9) | Yes |
| `ComputePipelines` (sub-API) | **Reserved, stub** | No (returns null in production) |
| Path Œ±/Œ≤ consistency analyzer (M3.5 deferred) | Future (per `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß11.2) | ‚Äî |
| FHE provider binding (`IHomomorphicComputeProvider`) | Reserved, dormant (¬ßD1 conditions) | ‚Äî |

### 8.5 Navigation-specific Mod API surface

There is **no `IModNavigationApi` sub-API.** A navigation-specific mod surface does not exist today; flow-field-based pathfinding (per G6/G7) ships through `IModFieldApi` (registering a `Vector2`-typed field) and `IModComputePipelineApi` (registering the distance + direction shaders). The intent is that the field API is general enough to carry navigation ‚Äî there is no separate navigation sub-API. This is consistent with `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 ¬ß"Mathematical isomorphism with electricity": same shader template, same field abstraction, different gameplay interpretation.

**Section structure (uniform across all 10):**

- `## Goal` (populated, 1 paragraph)
- `## Time estimate` (populated, "1 week" or "3-5 days")
- `## Deliverables (high-level)` (populated, bullet list, 3‚Äì6 items)
- `## Success criteria` (populated, 1‚Äì2 sentences)
- `## Status: NOT STARTED` (populated with prerequisite gate)

**What every G-skeleton lacks compared with `K8_34_COMBINED_KERNEL_CUTOVER_BRIEF_V2.md` (the post-Lesson-#7/#8 authored-shape):**

- **Phase-by-phase commit plan.** K8_34 v2 ships 4 commits with verbatim commit-message templates; G-skeletons specify only deliverable categories, not commit shapes.
- **Halt triggers / Stop-conditions.** K8_34 v2 ¬ß8 enumerates 8 halt conditions (SC-1 through SC-8). G-skeletons have no halt protocol.
- **Verification protocol.** K8_34 v2 ¬ß4.0 has mandatory deep-read instructions, ¬ß4.5 has migration-vs-rewrite thresholds, ¬ß7.4 has the green-test gate. G-skeletons have only `Success criteria`.
- **Closure protocol.** K8_34 v2 ¬ß9 enumerates REGISTER updates, CAPA closures, LOCKED-doc amendments. G-skeletons have no closure section.
- **API transcription** (Lesson #7 quality bar). K8_34 v2 ¬ß4 quotes signatures verbatim from on-disk files. G-skeletons reference architectural intent (`GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 ¬ßG{N}) but do not transcribe APIs because the APIs (e.g. `IModComputePipelineApi`) are stubs.
- **Atomic-state proof** (Lesson #8 quality bar). K8_34 v2 ¬ß1.4 walks through why Phase 4+5 is atomic. G-skeletons don't reason about commit atomicity.

**Recon classification:** All ten G-series briefs are skeleton-state. They are valuable architectural intent ‚Äî they make the GPU compute and movement direction explicit, in a form a deliberation can build on ‚Äî but **none is execution-ready under the post-A'.5 quality bar set by Lessons #7+#8.**

G5 (Domain B) is independent of the navigation chain ‚Äî it proves the entity-keyed compute pattern.

The forward-intent comments in `IsotropicDiffusionKernel.cs` ("for the future Vulkan compute shader (G-series)") are honest signposts; the stub interfaces are honest reservations; the capability strings are pre-wired manifest plumbing. No drift between intent and code in the sense of intent-claimed-implementation that does not exist; the only "drift" is the REGISTER classification (`lifecycle: AUTHORED` for skeleton-state briefs) per ¬ß3.3.
