## Section 10 ‚Äî Production systems map

`src/DualFrontier.Systems/` contains **28 `*System.cs` files** (sub-agent inventory). Of these, **10 are in the post-A'.5 `coreSystems` production array** (per K8.34 v2 brief ¬ß0.1 amendment, 12‚Üí10 with `ElectricGridSystem` + `ConverterSystem` deleted). The remaining 18 are `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = N)]` placeholder stubs awaiting Phase 5/6/7 implementation (the legacy Combat / Magic / Faction / World cycle, now subsumed into the M-series).

### 10.1 Production `coreSystems` (10 systems, post-A'.5)

| System | Tick rate | Reads | Writes | Touches navigation? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `NeedsSystem` | SLOW | (none) | `NeedsComponent` | No |
| `MoodSystem` | SLOW | `NeedsComponent` | `MindComponent` | No |
| `JobSystem` | NORMAL | `NeedsComponent`, `SkillsComponent`, **`PositionComponent`** | `JobComponent` | Yes (read) |
| `ConsumeSystem` | NORMAL | `NeedsComponent`, `JobComponent`, **`MovementComponent`**, **`PositionComponent`**, `WaterSourceComponent` | `ConsumableComponent` | Yes (read both nav components) |
| `SleepSystem` | NORMAL | `BedComponent`, `NeedsComponent`, `JobComponent`, **`PositionComponent`** | `BedComponent` | Yes (read) |
| `ComfortAuraSystem` | SLOW | `DecorativeAuraComponent`, **`PositionComponent`**, `NeedsComponent` | (none ‚Äî publishes events) | Yes (read) |
| **`MovementSystem`** | NORMAL | (none declared) | **`PositionComponent`**, **`MovementComponent`** | Yes (sole writer of both nav components) |
| `PawnStateReporterSystem` | SLOW | `NeedsComponent`, `MindComponent`, `JobComponent`, `IdentityComponent`, `SkillsComponent` | (none) | No |
| `InventorySystem` | FAST | (none) | `StorageComponent` | No |
| `HaulSystem` | NORMAL | `StorageComponent`, **`PositionComponent`**, `JobComponent` | (none ‚Äî publishes events) | Yes (read) |

**Recon-relevant facts:**

- **7 of 10 production systems read `PositionComponent`.** `MovementSystem` is the sole writer of `PositionComponent` and `MovementComponent`.
- **The dependency graph orders writes-after-reads-of-same-type structurally** ‚Äî so `MovementSystem` either runs after the 6 readers, or in a phase where they don't run. The actual phase assignment per the schedule is implicit (the readers are SLOW or NORMAL; `MovementSystem` is NORMAL; `TickRates.SLOW = 60`-tick interval, `NORMAL = 15`, etc.). At most every 60 ticks all 10 systems align; sequencing is by dependency-graph topological sort over reads/writes.
- **A navigation-foundation rework touches at least 7 systems** via `PositionComponent`. Plus `MovementSystem` for `MovementComponent` and `move.Path`.

### 10.2 `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = N)]` stubs (18 systems)

Per sub-agent's `[BridgeImplementation]` extraction, the stubs cluster by phase:

- **Phase 5 (Combat):** `CombatSystem`, `ComboResolutionSystem`, `CompositeResolutionSystem`, `DamageSystem`, `ProjectileSystem`, `StatusEffectSystem`
- **Phase 6 (Magic):** `CraftSystem`, `EtherGrowthSystem`, `GolemSystem`, `ManaSystem`, `RitualSystem`, `SpellSystem`
- **Phase 7 (Faction + World):** `RaidSystem`, `RelationSystem`, `TradeSystem`, `MapSystem`, `WeatherSystem`
- (`SkillSystem` is annotated Phase 3 ‚Äî see below)

The combined-grep sweep (¬ß13.3 below) shows these stubs are full of `// TODO: Phase N ‚Äî ‚Ä¶` markers documenting forward intent.

**Navigation-relevant stubs:**

- **`ProjectileSystem` (Phase 5 stub):** declared reads/writes `PositionComponent`. Per `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0, ProjectileSystem is the Domain B reactivation candidate (G5 milestone). Its current stub form is what G5 would replace.
- **`MapSystem` (Phase 7 stub):** `TODO: Phase 0 ‚Äî determine the area of interest, load/unload regions.` This is the **natural home for dynamic-map-expansion logic.** It exists, it is empty, and it is annotated Phase 7 ‚Äî which means it will not be addressed in the active K/A'/early-M cycle.

### 10.3 Dependency graph picture

Per sub-agent's analysis (cross-cutting Q3): the dependency graph operates on `HashSet<Type>` ‚Äî component types, not entity IDs or tiles. **For navigation, this means:**

- `MovementSystem` writes `PositionComponent` and `MovementComponent`. Every other system reading either type is constrained to run before or after `MovementSystem` in the topological order ‚Äî but not in the same phase.
- 7 reader-systems √ó 2 writer-types = a fan-in constraint: `MovementSystem` runs alone in its movement-write phase; readers run in earlier or later phases.
- **No spatial constraint expressible.** "This system only operates on tiles within radius R of pawn P" is not in the access language; it lives in event handler logic.

This is consistent with the field/ECS separation in `FIELDS.md` v0.1 ‚Äî and forward-compatible with G7 (`Vanilla.Movement`): the new `VelocityComponent` (or extended `MovementComponent`) becomes a new component type the graph can sequence on; the field reads (gradient lookups) are field-API, not graph-aware.

## Section 11 ‚Äî Game-assumption inventory (Layer A)

Per the brief's Layer A goal: identify where engine code is correctly abstracted vs prematurely specialized to current game assumptions. The sub-agent's game-assumption sweep produced the inventory below.

### 11.1 Map-scale assumptions

| File | Line | Constant | Value | Severity | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|---|
| `src/DualFrontier.Application/Loop/GameBootstrap.cs` | 45 | `MapWidth` | `200` | **High** | NavGrid/factory/spawn anchor. Single source of truth aspirationally; today, duplicated. |
| `src/DualFrontier.Application/Loop/GameBootstrap.cs` | 46 | `MapHeight` | `200` | **High** | Same. |
| `src/DualFrontier.Systems/Pawn/MovementSystem.cs` | 31 | `MapWidth` | `200` | **High** | **Duplicate of above**; used only for wander targets. Should consult shared config. |
| `src/DualFrontier.Systems/Pawn/MovementSystem.cs` | 32 | `MapHeight` | `200` | **High** | Same. |
| `src/DualFrontier.Presentation/Nodes/GameRoot.cs` | 20 | `MapWidth` | `200` | **High** | Third source of truth, presentation-side. |
| `src/DualFrontier.Presentation/Nodes/GameRoot.cs` | 21 | `MapHeight` | `200` | **High** | Same. |
| `src/DualFrontier.Core.Interop/CpuKernels/IsotropicDiffusionKernel.cs` | 20 | "200√ó200" (string) | ‚Äî | Low | Diagnostic comment only ‚Äî "40 000 P/Invokes per iteration on a 200√ó200 field". |
| `src/DualFrontier.Presentation/Nodes/GameRoot.cs` | 59 | "200√ó200√ó16" (string) | ‚Äî | Low | Diagnostic comment. |

**Layer A finding (High severity):** Map dimensions are hardcoded in **three independent source-of-truth locations**. The dynamic-map-expansion backlog item from `IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` is structurally incompatible with this shape; even static enlargement (e.g. `300√ó300`) requires three edits.

### 11.2 Pawn / colony scale

| File | Line | Constant | Value | Severity |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 47 | `InitialPawnCount` | `50` | Medium |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 50 | `ObstacleCount` | `800` | Medium |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 51 | `InitialFoodCount` | `150` | Medium |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 52 | `InitialWaterCount` | `50` | Medium |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 53 | `InitialBedCount` | `30` | Medium |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 54 | `InitialDecorationCount` | `25` | Medium |

**Layer A finding (Medium severity):** All initial-state counts are bootstrap constants. This is appropriate scope for a bootstrap class ‚Äî but a designer-driven scenario system (different starting conditions per game mode) would need to replace these with parameter input. None of the active G/K/A' briefs touch this layer.

### 11.3 Tick-rate constants

| File | Line | Constant | Value | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| `MovementSystem.cs` | 30 | `StepCooldownTicks` | `3` | 1 step per 4 ticks |
| `AStarPathfinding.cs` | 14 | `MaxIterations` | `2000` | A* iteration cap per query |

**Recon-relevant fact (Layer A medium severity):** Both are private to their system. A future variable-terrain-speed system would need to consult per-terrain modifiers, not a single `StepCooldownTicks` constant. The A\* iteration cap is reasonable for `200√ó200` (the grid has 40 000 cells; 2 000 is 5% of grid ‚Äî adequate for typical short paths, marginal for long obstacle-avoiding paths). On a hypothetical `1000√ó1000` grid the cap may need scaling ‚Äî but the relationship is not expressed; the constant doesn't move with map size.

### 11.4 System count and `coreSystems` array

| Location | Count | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| `GameBootstrap.cs` (post-A'.5 `coreSystems` array) | 10 | Post-power-deletion |
| `src/DualFrontier.Systems/` (`*System.cs` files) | 28 | Includes 18 `[BridgeImplementation]` stubs |
| `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = 5)]` | 6 | Combat |
| `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = 6)]` | 6 | Magic + Inventory CraftSystem |
| `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = 7)]` | 5 | Faction + World |
| `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = 3)]` | 1 | SkillSystem |

**Recon-relevant fact (Layer A low severity, but ambient):** The system count is in transition. The 10-system production set is the post-A'.5 stable shape; the 18 stubs represent prior-phase architectural commitments awaiting their M-series implementation. None of the stubs assert `200√ó200` map scale ‚Äî they are scope-stubs, not implementation-stubs.

### 11.5 Hardcoded seeds (RNG anchors)

| File | Line | Constant | Value |
|---|---:|---|---:|
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 48 | `FactorySeed` | `42` |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 49 | `ObstacleSeed` | `42` |
| `GameBootstrap.cs` | 55 | `ItemFactorySeed` | `43` |
| `Presentation/Nodes/TileMapRenderer.cs` | 23 | `DefaultSeed` | `42` |

Per `METHODOLOGY.md` ¬ß7.1 determinism invariant, deterministic replay requires every randomness source be reproducible. Today the seeds are co-located with their consumers ‚Äî adequate for the current single-player simulation but fragile.

The brief asked for `Seed = 42`, `Seed = 43`, and string identifiers like "200x200" / "200√ó200". All captured above. No `coreSystems`-as-identifier other than the constant `coreSystems` array name in `GameBootstrap` (no constant `CoreSystemCount`).

## Section 12 ‚Äî Drift inventory (LOCKED-doc vs code)

Sampled drift findings from documentation-vs-code cross-check. Per the brief: "sample-verify enough to characterize drift rate; do not exhaustively verify every claim".

### 12.1 KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md version mismatch

REGISTER reports `DOC-A-KERNEL` version `1.5`. On-disk frontmatter reads:

```yaml
version: "1.6"
last_modified: "2026-05-14"
last_modified_commit: "2df5921"  # in REGISTER
```

But the doc file's body header reads (verbatim from ¬ß4.1 above):

> **Version**: 1.6
> **Date**: 2026-05-14
> **Status**: AUTHORITATIVE LOCKED ‚Ä¶ A'.5 K8.3+K8.4 combined milestone closure 2026-05-14 ‚Äî managed `World` retired from production as `ManagedTestWorld`‚Ä¶

**Drift finding (Layer B, Low severity, expected):** REGISTER frontmatter is one version behind the on-disk doc. This is the expected lag ‚Äî REGISTER updates happen at milestone closure, and the A'.5 closure landed REGISTER updates (per commit `fc8ecb6`) but the schema metadata top-of-file (`schema_version`, `register_version`, `last_modified`) was not bumped. See ¬ß3.1.

### 12.2 ROADMAP.md staleness

`ROADMAP.md` v Live frontmatter reads `register_view_url: docs/governance/REGISTER_RENDER.md#DOC-C-ROADMAP`, status overview reads:

> *Updated: 2026-05-03 (M7 closed; M8 (Vanilla skeletons) is the next phase; ‚Ä¶)*.

This is **12 days stale** as of recon (2026-05-15). Between 2026-05-03 and 2026-05-15: K-series closure (K0‚ÄìK8), A'.0‚ÄìA'.5 closure (8 milestones), K9 closure (field storage shipped), the entire LOCKED-doc set updated. **The "Updated" line in the body has not moved.**

**Drift finding (Layer B, Medium severity):** ROADMAP `Status overview` line says "Updated: 2026-05-03" but the document records milestones through 2026-05-12+ in its body text. Either the "Updated" line is stale (and the table reflects current state), or both are stale. Recon does not edit it.

### 12.3 PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING.md duration estimate

`PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING.md` ¬ß3 reads:

| Phase | Estimate | Cumulative |
|---|---|---|
| A'.5 K8.3 | 4-6 weeks | 6-9 weeks |
| A'.6 K8.4 | 1-2 weeks | 7-11 weeks |

But the doc itself records (in its ¬ß2 body) that A'.5 absorbed K8.3+K8.4 into a single milestone shipped 2026-05-14. The ¬ß3 table reads as if K8.3 and K8.4 are still independent ‚Äî i.e. the table predates the K8.3+K8.4 combination decision.

**Drift finding (Layer B, Medium severity):** Internal contradiction between ¬ß2 (executive narrative, current) and ¬ß3 (duration table, pre-combination). Both halves of the same Live document; reads as a half-applied amendment.

### 12.4 KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md ¬ß1.1 project-structure tree

Sub-agent extract ‚Äî the doc records `DualFrontier.Core.Interop/Marshalling/SpanLease.cs`. On-disk: `SpanLease.cs` lives **top-level** at `src/DualFrontier.Core.Interop/SpanLease.cs`, not under `Marshalling/`. Same for `WriteBatch.cs`, `NativeWorld.cs`. The `Marshalling/` subfolder contains only `ComponentTypeRegistry.cs`, `EntityIdPacking.cs`, `NativeComponentType.cs`.

This is the **same drift the K8.1 closure surfaced as Phase 0.4 inventory deviation** (per `METHODOLOGY.md` "Phase 0.4 inventory as hypothesis, not authority" lesson). The doc was authored with the expected layout; the codebase converged on a different convention; the doc has not been amended. K8.34 v2 brief ¬ß0 carries the up-to-date layout; the LOCKED `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß1.1 has not been re-synced.

**Drift finding (Layer B, Low severity):** `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` ¬ß1.1 project-structure tree predates the K8.1 layout convergence. Convention-vs-document drift on file placement. No code impact (the convention is internally consistent), only documentation freshness.

### 12.5 NavGrid initial-state comment in `ROADMAP.md`

`ROADMAP.md` ¬ß"Phase 3 ‚Äî Pawns (closed)" reads:

> Pawns walk the map via A* pathfinding (2000-iteration cap, no cache). ‚Ä¶ `NavGrid` initialises from `GameBootstrap` (50√ó50, 50 obstacles).

But `GameBootstrap.cs:45‚Äì46` reads `MapWidth = 200, MapHeight = 200` and `:50` reads `ObstacleCount = 800`. The ROADMAP claim "50√ó50, 50 obstacles" describes an **earlier era** of the bootstrap (pre-current).

**Drift finding (Layer B, Medium severity):** ROADMAP ¬ß"Phase 3 ‚Äî Pawns" misreports current grid dimensions. The doc described the Phase 3 closure state; bootstrap has since scaled to 200√ó200, 800 obstacles. The ROADMAP entry is historical but reads as current ‚Äî a reader querying "what scale is the simulation today?" gets the wrong answer.

Stragglers in *code* comments are the same shape: defer to a cleanup pass.

The constraint may still hold for other reasons (synchronous A\* is simpler to reason about), but the documented rationale is stale.

## Section 13 ‚Äî IDEAS_RESERVOIR through the architectural lens

`docs/IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` v0.1 Live enumerates 9 post-release ideas. Per the document's ¬ß0: "An idea belongs here if implementing it would not require breaking any existing LOCKED spec." Recon's reading of each idea against current architecture:

### 13.1 Per-idea architectural fit

| ID | Idea | Architecture today supports? | What would require engine extension? | LOCKED-doc amendment needed? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ¬ß2.1 | AI mod assistant | Mostly. ALC isolation already covers generated mods; capability validation applies unchanged. | Local LLM dispatch surface; documentation-as-context tooling. | None for engine; potentially `MODDING.md` for documentation-as-context convention. |
| ¬ß2.2 | Voronoi-driven faction borders | No. Depends on GPU compute (Jump Flooding). | All of K9 (done) + G-series (G0‚ÄìG4 done would suffice for non-flow-field Voronoi); plus map-size-flexible field storage if borders are dynamic. | None ‚Äî `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 supports this domain class structurally. |
| ¬ß2.3 | TDA for social dynamics | Partial. P/Invoke surface exists; deterministic snapshot supports replay. | C++/C# GUDHI or Ripser bindings; event bus already carries social events. | None for engine; potentially `EVENT_BUS.md` for the new event surfaces. |
| ¬ß2.4 | Symbolic regression on simulation data | Partial. Subprocess/REST integration is mechanically possible; PySR via subprocess fits. | Subprocess management surface; simulation-trace serialization (depends on K-series determinism). | None. |
| ¬ß2.5 | Causal inference for player decisions | Partial. Deterministic snapshots support replay; ECS state is memcpy-snapshotable (per K7 evidence). | Snapshot management surface; counterfactual scheduler. | None (per `METHODOLOGY.md` ¬ß7.1 determinism invariant). |
| ¬ß2.6 | AI opponents through behavior cloning | Yes. Mods register systems; AI faction is one more mod. | Anonymized replay capture; small-transformer CPU inference (~50M params). | Privacy/consent decision is LOCKED-shaped (per the reservoir's own framing). |
| ¬ß2.7 | Lambda calculus REPL | Yes. Capability system gates every primitive op. | Evaluator (few hundred LOC). | None. |
| ¬ß2.8 | Player model for personalization | Yes. Event log captures actions; determinism guarantees rebuildability. | Local model serialization + UI surface. | None for engine; LOCKED decision on data boundary (export vs train-corpus) per the reservoir's own framing. |
| ¬ß2.9 | FHE integration | Architecturally **already ratified** at `FHE_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md` v1.0 LOCKED. | Library binding (pending ¬ßD1 conditions). | None ‚Äî contract is binding. |

### 13.2 The dynamic-map-expansion exception

Per `IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` ¬ß1:

> The current scope explicitly excludes everything in this document, with the single exception of dynamic map expansion, which lives in the active backlog.

But:

- **No occurrence of "dynamic map expansion" in `ROADMAP.md`** (sub-agent verified).
- **No occurrence in `PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING.md`** (recon-verified).
- **No brief authored** for the feature.
- **No LOCKED-doc commitment** beyond `IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` itself.
- **The architectural inventory (¬ß5C.2)** shows ‚â•9 subsystems would need changes for dynamic expansion; none has scoped or accepted the work.

**Recon finding (Layer A + Layer C, high severity):** The "active backlog" claim is not realized in any active artifact. This is a **structural placeholder lie at the documentation governance layer** ‚Äî the reservoir document asserts existence of a backlog item that no other artifact reflects. Per METHODOLOGY ¬ß7.1 seventh invocation (every `.md` either has a register entry or is out-of-scope), the **claim itself has a register entry** (the reservoir doc) but the **claim's referent** (the active backlog) has no register entry. The reservoir document references the active backlog as if it were a documented surface; the active backlog as documented surface does not exist. Either the reservoir should not claim the exception until the backlog item is operationalized, or an artifact reifying the backlog item should be authored.

### 13.3 Cross-feature combinations relevant to navigation

`IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` ¬ß3 enumerates 6 combinations. The combination most relevant to navigation:

- **¬ß2.2 + ¬ß2.3 (Voronoi + TDA):** "Geographic and social topology together." Both depend on GPU compute infrastructure (G-series). Both depend on a map-size-flexible substrate (Voronoi cells "extend beyond the visible region without special-casing" ‚Äî explicit dynamic-map requirement). Both are post-G-series-completion. **Implication:** The dynamic-map-expansion question is upstream of both these ideas; resolving it unblocks two reservoir entries.

**Recon classification:** The TODOs are **scope markers**, not debt. They identify code shells (Phase 5/6/7 stub systems and stub events) awaiting their M-cycle implementation. Per `METHODOLOGY.md` ¬ß7.1, an empty stub with `TODO: Phase N` is **on the edge** of the data-exists-or-it-doesn't principle ‚Äî it has an attached scope marker, but the body is `// TODO`. The Phase-4 closure (Phase 4 housekeeping debt closure) replaced `NotImplementedException` stubs with empty bodies + `[BridgeImplementation(Phase = N)]` (per `ROADMAP.md` ¬ß"v0.3 architectural fixes"). The current TODOs are this shape ‚Äî scoped, attributed, awaiting implementation.

**Severity: Informational.** **Proposed window:** each TODO closes in its named phase; recon does not propose a sweep.

## Section 15 ‚Äî Synthesis

> Sections 1‚Äì14 are factual; this section is synthesis. Recon's reading of what the facts mean for the next architectural deliberation. **No specific architectural designs.** Only: what questions does the user now have evidence to answer; what questions remain open; what should next deliberation prioritize.

### 15.1 What the facts say (consolidated)

The recon assembled three converging pictures.

**Picture 1 ‚Äî Where the engine is correctly abstracted.** The kernel layer (K-series) is mature. `NativeWorld` is the sole production storage path; the C ABI is stable across 12 sub-domains (~58 functions); K9 field storage adds a peer abstraction to component storage; the Mod API v3 surface is extensible by design (additive sub-APIs, null-degraded on missing builds). The architecture-vs-game-assumption boundary is **clean** at the kernel level ‚Äî `NativeWorld`, `RawComponentStore`, `RawTileField` know nothing about `200√ó200`, `50` pawns, or `coreSystems` count. The scheduler is configurable; the dependency graph operates on types; the bus surface is mod-defined.

**Picture 2 ‚Äî Where the engine is prematurely specialized.** The specialization sits **above the kernel**, in two thin layers:

- **`NavGrid` and the pathfinding service** specialize to one fixed-size grid allocated at bootstrap. The grid is parametric in `(width, height)` but the constants are duplicated across `GameBootstrap` (line 45‚Äì46), `MovementSystem` (line 31‚Äì32), and `GameRoot` (presentation layer, line 20‚Äì21). The A\* iteration cap (2000) is implicitly tuned to `200√ó200` and won't scale automatically. The path is consumed via per-pawn synchronous A\* ‚Äî adequate for 50 pawns on a 200√ó200 grid, structurally fragile for 200+ pawns or larger grids.
- **`GameBootstrap` is the single point where game scale is decided.** Map dimensions, pawn count, obstacle count, item counts, RNG seeds ‚Äî all `private const int`. This is appropriate for the current single-scenario bootstrap shape, but means changing scale requires touching one file (good) that propagates to two other duplicates (bad), and that **dynamic** changes have no path through the surface.

**Picture 3 ‚Äî Where the forward intent sits.** The G-series skeleton briefs (G0‚ÄìG9) and `GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 commit to a navigation architecture that **dissolves the current shape**: pathfinding becomes a `Vanilla.Movement` mod that registers `Vector2`-typed flow fields, dispatches distance + direction compute shaders, reads gradient via per-pawn P/Invoke, and falls back to A\* only for unique-destination paths. The K-L9 "vanilla = mods" principle commits navigation to mod-side ownership. The architectural compound effect ‚Äî pathfinding cost decouples from pawn count ‚Äî is the documented "moat" claim in `GPU_COMPUTE.md` ¬ß"Pathfinding scaling".

**The forward intent's prerequisites are not yet sequenced.** G7 (`Vanilla.Movement`) requires G6 (flow field infra) requires G0 (Vulkan compute plumbing) requires M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 (Vulkan runtime). M9.0‚ÄìM9.4 is `‚è≠ Pending` per `ROADMAP.md` with no slot in the active A'-cycle. The K-series finishes A'.6‚ÄìA'.9; M-cycle resumes M8.4+; runtime M9.x is parallel to the M-cycle. The G-series sits beyond the visible horizon of the A'-cycle.

**Picture 4 ‚Äî Where the architecture is honest about not being there yet.** The compute-pipeline API surface is a stub. `IModComputePipelineApi` declares one property; the LOCKED spec commits to two methods. `RestrictedModApi.ComputePipelines => null`. Mods are told to check for null and degrade. **This is the right shape for forward intent ‚Äî it commits the namespace, reserves the name, leaves the surface unbound until G0.** Recon would have surfaced this as a finding had it been masquerading as functional; the architecture is explicit about the placeholder. Same shape as `FHE_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md` v1.0 ‚Äî ratify the principle, defer the binding.

### 15.2 What the user has evidence to answer

The brief framed the recon's deliverable as **ground truth** input to a future deliberation. The recon's findings let the deliberation answer several questions concretely:

1. **Is the architecture sound, or does it need broad rework?** The kernel architecture is sound. The scheduler architecture is sound. The Mod API extension model is sound. The drift is at the **navigation layer** and at the **dynamic-map-expansion seam** ‚Äî both above the kernel.
2. **What is the gap between LOCKED-doc commitments and code?** Drift is **low-to-medium** at the layer recon sampled (¬ß12). The most consequential drift is REGISTER staleness post-A'.5 closure and ROADMAP staleness against current milestone state ‚Äî neither is functional drift. The `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` v1.6 / REGISTER v1.5 mismatch is the predictable closure-lag pattern.
3. **What is the gap between forward intent (G-series) and code?** Pure forward delta. Zero G-skeleton concept names appear in `src/`. The skeleton briefs identify what code will exist; they do not over-claim that any of it exists.
4. **Is the navigation foundation ready for dynamic map expansion?** No. Three independent map-scale source-of-truth locations (¬ß11.1). `NavGrid` is allocation-only at bootstrap. No `MapSystem` implementation. No dynamic-map-expansion brief, despite the `IDEAS_RESERVOIR.md` claim that it is in active backlog.
5. **Is the navigation foundation ready for the hybrid pathfinding intent?** No, but for known reasons. The path from "per-pawn A\* on 200√ó200" to "hybrid flow field + A\* fallback via `Vanilla.Movement`" is the G6/G7 path, and G6/G7 are five skeleton-state milestones away from execution.
6. **Is the architecture mod-extensible for navigation?** Today: weakly. `IPathfindingService` lives in `DualFrontier.AI` (internal-shaped). There is no `IModNavigationApi` surface. Per K-L9 + G7 intent, navigation moves to mod-side via `Vanilla.Movement`, using `IModApi.Fields` + `IModApi.ComputePipelines`. The architecture commits this; the code is upstream.

### 15.3 What questions remain open

Recon records observations and questions; it does not propose solutions. The following questions are surfaced by the facts but not answered:

1. **Should dynamic map expansion be operationalized in the current cycle, or after the G-series lands?** The IDEAS_RESERVOIR claim of "active backlog" is unrealized; recon doesn't know whether the claim was aspirational or whether a brief is in flight elsewhere. Resolving this question changes what `NavGrid`, `GameBootstrap`, `MapSystem`, and the field storage layer need to look like.
2. **Should the navigation foundation be ratified now (as a contract, dormant pending G6/G7) or built when G-series lands?** The FHE contract precedent (¬ß4.7) shows the project's existing pattern for ratify-now-build-later. The G-series skeletons are similar in spirit ‚Äî but they're skeletons, not authored contracts under FHE's discipline. A navigation contract that locks the hybrid pathfinding model + dynamic-expansion compatibility + mod-extension surface, dormant pending G7 activation, is one shape the deliberation could consider.
3. **Should the three hardcoded `MapWidth`/`MapHeight` source-of-truth locations be consolidated now, or deferred to dynamic-expansion work?** Consolidation is cheap (introduce `IWorldDimensions` or `MapConfig`, route the three readers through it). Deferring means re-doing the consolidation as part of dynamic-expansion work ‚Äî but the consolidation is a prerequisite for dynamic-expansion anyway, so doing it now is path-clearing rather than path-extending. **Recon does not propose either choice.**
4. **Should the G-series briefs be re-classified in REGISTER from `AUTHORED` to a new `SKELETON` lifecycle (or analog)?** This is a `FRAMEWORK.md` v1.0 question. Recon surfaces the classification mismatch as a finding (¬ß14.1.e) without proposing the fix.
5. **Should `MovementSystem` be moved to a `Vanilla.Movement` mod assembly now (as a prerequisite for G7 authoring), or wait for G-series activation?** Per K-L9, `MovementSystem` is engine-shape today and mod-shape tomorrow. The M-cycle vanilla skeleton phase (M8) is `‚è≠ Pending`. Moving navigation early is an M-cycle scope question, not a navigation-architecture question.
6. **Does the IModComputePipelineApi placeholder surface need to be broadened before G0?** The current stub (1 property) doesn't match the LOCKED-doc surface (2 methods). A G0 brief author would need to either widen the interface (with mock implementation) or accept the gap.
7. **How should `MapSystem` (the Phase-7 stub) relate to dynamic-map-expansion?** It is currently empty and Phase-7-attributed. Its TODO reads "Phase 0 ‚Äî determine the area of interest, load/unload regions" ‚Äî i.e. chunk management, which is the natural home for expansion logic.
8. **Should the recon report itself be enrolled in REGISTER as `DOC-E-ARCHITECTURE_RECON_REPORT`?** This deliverable carries a frontmatter that mirrors the precedent (`DOC-E-CPP_KERNEL_BRANCH_REPORT`). REGISTER itself is not edited by recon (brief prohibits it). A subsequent governance action would enroll the report.

### 15.4 What the next deliberation might prioritize

This subsection is **interpretation**, not direction. Three observations the user may find load-bearing:

- **The dynamic-map-expansion question is the gating ambiguity.** It is the one named active-backlog item from the reservoir; it intersects with the G-series (variable-size flow fields), with `MapSystem` (chunk management), with `NavGrid` (resize), with the duplicated map-size constants (¬ß11.1), and with the LOCKED-doc surface (no `MapConfig` / `IWorldDimensions` exists in any LOCKED spec today). Resolving its scope and authoring shape ‚Äî whether ratified as a contract now or scoped to a specific milestone ‚Äî would clarify the priority of the surrounding deltas.

- **The G-series briefs are the right primary artifact but the wrong shape.** They are skeleton-state forward intent; the project's authored-brief discipline (post-Lessons #7+#8) requires phase-by-phase commit plans, halt protocols, verification gates. **An authoring pass on G0 ‚Äî the entry point of the chain ‚Äî would surface whether the dependencies (M9.0‚ÄìM9.4, K9 already done, the compute pipeline API surface) are in fact ready.** A G0 authoring pass is itself a brief-authoring deliberation; it would expose either (a) the surface is ready and execution can proceed, or (b) prerequisites need to be re-scoped (e.g. the runtime M9.x cycle picked up).

- **Navigation deserves a LOCKED contract before implementation pressure begins.** The FHE precedent shows the project ratifies binding decisions when the world is unknowable. Today the navigation surface is unknowable in implementation specifics (which RVO variant? which Eikonal solver? which LRU policy?) but **specifiable in principle**: the hybrid model, the per-pawn cost decoupling, the mod-side ownership, the engine-provided substrate, the dynamic-expansion compatibility. A navigation contract that locks principle now and defers binding to G7 would protect the deliberation from the discovery ‚Äî at G7 authoring time ‚Äî that one of the principle decisions doesn't fit. The pattern works because the principle is specifiable now; the architectural intent is concrete (`GPU_COMPUTE.md` v2.0 + G6/G7 skeletons); only the specifics await measurement.

These are three **observations the facts support**, not recommendations the recon imposes. The deliberation that reads this report holds the architectural judgment; recon holds the evidence.

### 15.5 What the recon is not saying

- The recon does not say the architecture is broken. It is not.
- The recon does not say the G-series briefs are wrong. They are skeletons; they record valid forward intent.
- The recon does not say the 200√ó200 hardcoding is a bug. It is a current-scale specialization that has not yet been generalized; whether to generalize it is a deliberation question.
- The recon does not say dynamic map expansion must happen in the next milestone. It says the claim "active backlog" is unrealized in artifacts ‚Äî whether to realize it is a deliberation question.
- The recon does not say navigation needs a LOCKED contract. It says the FHE precedent exists; whether to apply it to navigation is a deliberation question.
- The recon does not say `MovementSystem` should be moved to a mod assembly now. The K-L9 invariant says it will be; recon doesn't say when.

The recon's job is to make the evidence available. The evidence is here.
